Hello
I have joined the Optimal Reset the best I could but I have a question:

I have had problems for years with hypothyroidism and increased Erfa Thyroid with Â½ grain to 4Â½ grain in october or november last year (I cant remember the exact time). Below are my testresults:

July 2012 4 grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 1,00 (1,2-2,8)
T4 17,8 0 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

December 2012 4Â½ grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 2,01 (1,2-2,8)
T4 = 19,8 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

Febuary 2013 4Â½ grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 2,98 (1,2-2,8)
T4 = 25,1 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

I have only changed 2 things since the bloodtest in december:
1\. the 25th january started drinking 1Â½ liter of water with seaweed every day. I put it in the refrigreator in the evening.
2\. The 25th january I cut my BAB to HAB

Do you think that it is the seaweed that has changed the numbers or am I finally making progress with the medicine (Erfa Tyroid) after taking it since october 2010?

### Inger — 2013-03-12T08:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80161>

No Jasmin, I do not filter my water because we have our own well here, it comes from 30 meter deep. It is not a well that flows up from the ground itself, which would be the best.. but it gets pumped up with a pump. It is not filtered though and nothing added either, and it tastes great! I take care to always let the water flow quite a bit before I fill my glass jugs for the day. I want it fresh from the earth, not water that have been stuck in the tubes for long.

I just realized the water gets a way stronger "taste of sun" when it put it outside in a glass bowl instead of a jug!!! way different! I bet it is because it has a large surface to suck up the sunbeams directly, not through a glass... told me something about glass isolating pretty well..... from the goodness of the sun.. wow it really was a difference! So I need to buy some more big glass bowls like this...

About seaweeds.
I made some Dulce seaweed water the other day first time ever.. Dulce is very high Iodine. I realized yesterday I have a weird bump on my one finger... I assume it is from a bit too much Iodine? I got me some Brazil nuts.. and ate a handful yesterday after work..lol I will drink some saltwater today too. I think it is possible to get detox with seaweeds too, without doubt! It is good to check the Iodine content of the different brands because it varies huge. It could be a issue if one is drinking much from the high Iodine ones...

Jasmin, if no possibility to buy seaweeds from cleaner sources I do think also Baltic Sea seaweeds could be healthy, but I would do that only if no other option. But I bet it is better that nothing! I do eat fish from Baltic Sea too once in a while... I do try to limit it though.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T20:37:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80224>

Inger, you can taste the sun in your water? Amazing.
Yes, I imagine a water pump would disturb the water.

I got a small bump on my finger, from accidentally drinking iodine water (Lugols) that I thought was regular water. I drank way more than I normally would. The next day I got the bump. That was a few months ago. I still have it, even though now I can drink about the same amount of iodine water I accidentally ingested that day, with no bad effects.

The bump hurts, if I press on it or bump it. I think it might be bromide. Wish I knew how to get rid of it. I would guess that you are overdoing the seaweed water.

I have found the iodine level in different types of seaweed:
In 100 gram there is:
Nori = 5 mg
Wakame = 15 mg
Dulse = 20 mg
Arame = 60 mg
Combu =340 mg (I know this level looks like it is a joke )

The level can be different from season and where it is harvested.

I dont know what to listen for if I am getting too much iodine - do you know the signs?

I know that some warn against iodine if one has truble with the metabolism but I havent found out why yet. But if Jack says that seaweed can get me off the medicine then I believe him

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T02:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80924>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=80161)
>
> I just realized the water gets a way stronger "taste of sun" when it put it outside in a glass bowl instead of a jug!!! way different! I bet it is because it has a large surface to suck up the sunbeams directly, not through a glass... told me something about glass isolating pretty well..... from the goodness of the sun.. wow it really was a difference!

Oh, this is great!! I had access to well water this weekend, and so I put some in a soup bowl out in the sun. It really changes the flavor! Makes it "mellow", in my opinion. I tried it several times, in the course of a day, and found that just a few minutes in the sun, less than 5 min, is enough, if the sun is high. The water would still be cool, but remarkably different than it tasted a few minutes before. There would be small bubbles on the surface of the bowl.

I also left some out for a couple hours, and I actually didn't like the taste. The next day was cloudy, and though I tried it again, I didn't get the same effect. The water tasted much the same as from the tap. Sun water needs sun!

Great discovery, Inger. Thanks!

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-10T10:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-126164>

I am having trouble finding a good seaweed company that is a one stop shop. I have looked at some of these but they don't have a comprehensive line of seaweeds. I would love if someone made a product and blended together these seaweeds. I clipped this from Jack's blog Brain/Gut 6
When eating non ocean bearing fats/protein consider adding Sea Veggies to the recipe:

A. **Irish Moss** (chondrus crispus, carrageen) is full of electrolyte minerals — calcium, magnesium, sodium and potassium. Its mucilaginous compounds help you detoxify, boost metabolism and strengthen hair, skin and nails. In Eastern medicine it is traditionally used for a low sex drive because it helps support T3 levels to convert LDL to progesterone and not to cortisol.

B. **Wakame** (alaria, undaria) is a high-protein, high calcium seaweed, with carotenes, iron and vitamin C. It is used in Chinese medicine for hair growth and luster, and for skin tone because it optimizes thyroid function.

C. **Kelp** (laminaria) contains vitamins the fat soluble vitamins A, B, E, D and K in high quantity, and is a major source of vitamin C from the sea. It also happens to be rich in many minerals found in land based plants. This is why it made [my top ten supplement list](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/) so long ago in the blog series. Kelp proteins are high quality and present in abundance for a sea plant. It is a brown marine plant, kelp contains sodium alginate (algin), an element that helps remove radioactive particles (think Fukishima Iodine) and heavy metals from the body. Kelp can works as a blood purifier, relieves arthritis stiffness, and promotes adrenal, pituitary and thyroid health. Kelp’s natural iodine can normalize thyroid-related disorders like obesity and muscle fatigue seen in fibromyalgia cases. **Clinical pearl:**people with herpes virus of any type: It is a demulcent that helps eliminate herpes outbreaks after they have occurred. Kelp is nutrient rich and a small amount gives a large clinical result often.

D. **Hijiki** is a mineral-rich, high-fiber seaweed, with 20% protein, vitamin A, carotenes and calcium. Hijiki has the **most calcium** of any sea green, 1400mg per 100 grams of dry weight.

E. **Kombu** (laminaria digitata, setchelli, horsetail kelp), has a long tradition as a Japanese delicacy with reported nutritional healing value. Natural healers use it is a decongestant for excess mucous and to helps normalize blood pressure. Kombu has abundant iodine, carotenes, B, C, D and E vitamins, minerals like calcium, magnesium, potassium, silica, iron and zinc, and the powerful skin healing nutrient germanium, which is a pretty rare element in the human diet. Kombu is a meaty, high-protein seaweed. It is higher in natural mineral salts than most other seaweeds. I often add a strip of kombu to my bone broths and seafood broths.

F. **Nori** (porphyra, laver) is a red sea plant with a sweet, meaty taste when dried. It contains nearly 50% balanced, assimilable protein, higher than any other sea plant. Nori’s fiber makes it popular for sushi wrapping. I do not eat any rice because its miRNA directly affects our DNA expression. Nori is rich in all the carotenes, calcium, iodine, iron, and phosphorus.

G. **Sea Palm** (Postelsia Palmaeformis), American arame, grows only on the Pacific Coast of North America by Oregon and Washington State. It has a honeyed, salty taste that makes it tasty vegetable. I like it with my cauliflower rice recipe form the [e-cookbook](http://www.jackkruse.com/optimized-cooking/), or as a summer or autumn salad topping.

H. **Bladderwrack** is loaded with vitamin K and it is an excellent adrenal adaptogen and it helps sensitize us to insulin to because of its K2 like effects. It is often used today by Native American cultures broths and in sauna/steam baths for degenerative arthritis and inflammatory joint conditions.

I. **Dulse** (palmaria palmata), a red sea plant, very rich in iron. The last two sea veggies are my favorites because they have the most iodine of any plants on our planet. I was made aware of this sea vegetable when I was researching the Geritol solution for global warming. It also has abundant protein, and vitamin A. What really makes it a specially balanced nutrient for a lactovegetarian paleo diet is that it contains 300 times more iodine and 50 times more iron than wheat products. Tests on dulse show antiviral action against the herpes virus.

J.**Arame** (Eisenia bycyclis), is one of the ocean’s richest sources of iodine. It often contains **more iodine than seafood does**! Because of its high iodine content it can have major effects on women’s progesterone to estradiol ratio’s naturally. If you are estrogen dominant you might consider using this as become a staple in your broths and recipes. Many natural healers use arame to help reduce breast and uterine fibroids, excessive bleeding, fibrocystic disease of the breasts, and ovarian cysts. It is also quite helpful in cases of PCOS with acne and excessive facial hair. It also contains a lot fat soluble vitamins and phytohormones, to normalize peri menopausal and menopausal symptoms. Arame is often associated with soft wrinkle-free skin turgor and tends to enhances hair growth and make your hair have an incredible sheen. The reason is because this veggie increase your free T3 to a dramatic degree when it is used regularly in your diet.

On the subject of seaweed, I snipped this little piece from somewhere in the blog and saved it as a "note to self". I don't know which blog post it is from. But I thought it would connect well with the topic of seaweed.
Yes.......T3 has three iodine atoms

"most of the moisturizers women use are estrogen creators and by definition......iodine depleters........when your Prog/E2 levels are off you iodine usually sucks.......and this results in high LDL and low free T3. Low T3 means a low 2 hydroxylase and 16 hydroxylase issue for estrogens.......this further makes your prog/E2 worse."

I am guessing if women are suffering the low progesterone blues, something like arame would be good. BTW, Korea is a great source of seaweed. I lived there for 17 years but wouldn't eat the seaweed that was everywhere. Now, I am getting my Korean friend to bring it to me. She is a personal shopper and could send you some of my seaweed order. I am going to really mix it up to get a good blend. Let me know if you would like her information.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-08T15:22:13+0200

I drank some pelligrino yesterday - meh? It tasted nice... but the best spring water I've ever had was up at trinity lake california - it was called "Shirley Springs - and it bubbled up right out of the ground - you'd pull your boat up to the island the spring was located - take up your containers, and fill it up. the National Forest service - figured out how to put a dispenser hose in the spring - so you could fill up the containers...

just freaking awesome...

I also love a bottled water I'd get when I lived in ID - it had the sun valley sun on it - but I forget the name... going to check whole foods and see if I can find it again...

I am going to try other waters as well...

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T15:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79795>

For two years, I have been drinking Fiji water, Apollinaris, & WF 365 brand water, which is just Crystal Geyser sourced from a spring in the Appalachian mountains that is completely untouched by man above the source.

When I'm pregnant, I can't drink still water. I don't know why. It must be sparkling. My pregnant self would accept club soda, but not still water, and strongly preferred Apollinaris.

When I'm not, I can drink still water. I always look for "that taste" - the taste of the water I grew up on, which was from a well in Georgia. While little else was right about my upbringing, health-wise, the water was clean & tasted good. When I first tasted real spring water, I knew what I had been missing ever since moving to "city water" - the minerals! I started buying ConcenTrace and adding it to my water till it had "that taste."

I started buying a lot of FIji, but was uncomfortable with flying it around the planet when the people in Fiji don't even really get much of it. Now I buy just one liter/wk of that and use it whenever I'm upstairs. I think that's probably a good move in an additional way - the app someone posted from Gerolsteiner's website that compares mineral content of waters shows that FIji is very, very high in silicon (that's why it feels so smooth), but little else. Apollinaris is leagues higher in Mg & many other trace minerals, higher even than the Gerolsteiner that the app was made to push!

The CG water also has a good taste (good indicator of mineral content), but I still add ConcenTrace, b/c my whole family drinks it as our main water source, and I know we are all mineral deprived. They don't all yet eat as much seafood as I do, and I know our soils are so depleted that even organic food & water no longer have enough minerals.

I was thinking about getting a Berkey filter, top of the line with fluoride filter, too, and using it for our tap water (it would end up at $.05/gallon), which comes from the Chattahoochee River (which starts as a mountain stream in the Appalachians, but then goes through Lake Lanier and gets boated in & floats around, probably for years, till it comes out through the dam!), but I think I've decided to stay with CG for now. $.80/gallon when I buy it by the case at WF.

BTW, I'm still dehydrated, lol. Horrible Bun/creatinine ratio & getting worse (has gotten worse every lab I've taken for the 1.5yrs since starting the LR), wake up every morning with bloody sinus stuff coming out my nose (this started around the time I started the LR), & massive water retention across my whole body that fluctuates up and down 10lb in just a few days, often without me understanding what the cause is.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T17:20:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79802>

All water naturally has fluoride. It's an element. Some has more than others, depending on the mineral content of the surrounding area, but it's a \*trace\* mineral in natural water, in \*trace\* amounts. The question is, does it have industrial waste (sodium fluoride) added to it? That's the real hazard.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T19:50:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79822>

Wait - is someone trying to say there is water somewhere on earth that is naturally occurring and has \*no\* fluoride in it? Granted, I haven't read every single word on this forum, but I missed that.

From wikipedia: "Fresh water supplies generally contain between **0.01–0.3 ppm**, whereas the ocean contains between **1.2 and 1.5 ppm**. (Not that anyone here is drinking ocean water, but YKWIM.) ... In some locations, the fresh water contains dangerously high levels of fluoride, leading to serious health problems."

Obviously in natural water that naturally contains excessive levels, you have a problem. But is anyone saying they have a source that \*naturally\*, without any kind of filtering, has <.01ppm fluoride in it?

Don't forget to compare apples to apples. Governing bodies almost always allow groups to round down to zero if they have below a certain amount. Filtered municipal water that is smack dab in the middle of "normal" for fresh water, say at .08mg/L (which is .08ppm), could easily be rounded down and reported as zero, when it actually has just as much as most natural sources of water.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T19:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79824>

I just checked that website, and it appears to be reporting almost every non-fluoridated municipal supply as "zero." That just means they're rounding down. They're simply not getting specific to that degree. All natural water, to my knowledge, has fluoride in it, in the range given at wikipedia. I'd be very interested in information showing that there is any naturally occurring water with fluoride at a significantly lower concentration.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-09T21:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79846>

I must say that I think it is funny that most everyone believes what it says on the bottle as far as mineral content. Unless you see an current analysis from a certified lab I would have a low level of confidence in any number I saw on a label. I have gotten distilled water at the supermarket for car batteries and found that it had more minerals in it than my RO kitchen water as tested by a water conductivity meter. Better yet test fluoride for yourself if you really care - [LINK](http://www.extech.com/Instruments/product.asp?catid=71&prodid=424). Or send your own sample to a lab. It is not that expensive. The metering of chemicals in drinking water systems is not high tech especially in smaller facilities which means that sh\*t happens sometimes and the level is not close what it is suppose to be - higher or lower. And those chemicals they use are note exactly pharmaceutical grade and often of a lower quality from Asia. I am looking into bone char filters to deal with the fluoride in our domestic water.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-08T15:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-79656>

So Last night I had the best sleep ever! OMG! read stories, nursed Havoc, brushed everyone's teeth - laid down w/the kids... 900pm... by 915 I WAS OUT! I woke up around 11pm, got up brushed my teeth, popped in my OSA Sleep device, and applied my Pg BHRT crawled into our bed by 1115 and was out in under 10mins. when I woke up - to my alarm - it was like amazing how I felt. WOW is all I can say. I did some serious grounding yesterday - on the way to work, at lunch, on the way home, and playing with the kids and the dog in the back yard.

going to try to do the same thing today... and get in the CT Tank as well..

What I can't figure out is if the good sleeping is a combination of several things or just one thing. We've been using the magnetico sleep pad for almost a month, and I've been focusing on grounding for almost an entire month. I'm not to the point that this is an every night occurrence but its happening more often than not.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-08T22:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-79703>

It seems that the PPP does more than make ribose and reduce glutathione. Maybe increased endogenous hormones are kicking in.

"Enzymes that function primarily in the reductive direction utilize the NADP+/NADPH cofactor pair as co-factors
as opposed to oxidative enzymes that utilize the NAD+/NADH cofactor pair. The reactions of fatty acid
biosynthesis and **steroid biosynthesis utilize large amounts of NADPH**. As a consequence, cells of the liver,
adipose tissue, adrenal cortex, testis and lactating mammary gland have high levels of the PPP enzymes. In fact
**30% of the oxidation of glucose in the liver occurs via the PPP.** Additionally, erythrocytes utilize the reactions of the
PPP to generate large amounts of NADPH used in the reduction of glutathione (see below). The conversion of
ribonucleotides to deoxyribonucleotides (through the action of ribonucleotide reductase) requires NADPH as the
electron source, therefore, any rapidly proliferating cell needs large quantities of NADPH."
\-themedicalbiochemistrypage.org-

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-12T03:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-80138>

When you eat a ketogenic diet guess what else happens.......we bypass complex one at the inner mitochondrial membrane and enter FADH2. You heard about this in the Quantum electron post.........but what did not I tell you? Why does a ketogenic diet work in T1D, T2D, and to lose weight? When you lose water conduction at the inner mitochondrial membrane you lose the ability of proper nanoscopic protein folding of cytochrome 1. This throws off the nanoscopic precision required for quantum tunneling and this cause increased ROS and metabolic syndrome........This is also why niacin works in these diseases because it is a ketone mimic drug. Why? Niacin also known as vitamin B3, nicotinic acid and vitamin PP, is an organic compound with the formula C6H5NO2 and one of the 40 to 80 essential human nutrients. In fact, when niacin deficiency is present it is one of 5 vitamins that causes a pandemic disease condition called pellagra. But it can be used to bypass a "broken" cytochrome 1 when mis-folding (due to a chronic ATP deficiency think EMF 7). But why kathylu? Why am I tormenting you with this science,.......because you are biochemist........and you will see where QED takes us..........Vitamin B3 can help activate PPP when we are broken for some reason when we are ketotic from a dietary stand point using vast amounts of coherent water as I laid out in the quantum electron blog post. ( ) Yes, kathylu, niacin is a precursor to NAD+/NADH (cytochrome 1) and NADP+/NADPH (the magic of the PPP), which play essential metabolic roles in all living cells as I showed in EMF 4. Niacin cannot be directly converted to nicotinamide, but both compounds could be converted to NAD and NADP in vivo and this information is very valuable when you have liver leptin resistance, metabolic syndrome, or epilepsy. When cytochrome 1 has mis-folded electron transport proteins (quinolone issue of CoEnQ10) you need to bypass it 100% of the time by eating fats predominately because if you don't, your mitochondria begin to make massive ROS that overwhelms the cell lowers vitamin D and all hormones, causes pregnenolone steal syndrome, and eventually shortens its telomeres to cause cellular signaling problems that lead to either senesence or cancer. The reason why? NAD+ builds up in relation to NADH levels on a per unit basis. Increasing the NAD+ in comparison to NADH changes a most of the mechanistic downstream signalling in protein kinase B pathways of cells. Why is that big? The AKT pathway is also known as protein kinase B (PKB). It is a serine/threonine-specific protein kinase that plays a key role in multiple cellular processes such as **glucose metabolism, apoptosis, cell proliferation, transcription and cell migration.** Akt1 is involved in cellular survival pathways, by inhibiting apoptotic processes. Akt1 is also able to induce protein synthesis pathways, and is therefore a key signaling protein in the cellular pathways that lead to skeletal muscle hypertrophy, and general tissue growth. Since it can block apoptosis, and thereby promote cell survival, Akt1 has been implicated as a major factor in many types of cancer. Akt (now also called Akt1) was originally identified as the oncogene in the transforming retrovirus, AKT8. In a mouse which is null for Akt1 but normal for Akt2, glucose homeostasis is unperturbed, but the animals are smaller, consistent with a role for Akt1 in growth. In contrast, mice which do not have Akt2, but have normal Akt1, have mild growth deficiency and display a diabetic phenotype (insulin resistance/ T2D), again consistent with the idea that Akt2 is more specific for the insulin receptor signaling pathway. The role of Akt3 is less clear, though it appears to be predominantly expressed in the brain. It has been reported that mice lacking Akt3 have small brains. This is what causes brain shrinkage in all the neurodegenerative disorders that are linked to cytochrome 1 issues. PCOS is tied to this early one before the more chronic diseases come to the forefront.
Akt2 is an important signaling molecule in the Insulin signaling pathway. It is required to induce glucose transport.

The follow through? AKT1 is involved in the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway and other signaling pathways. These all cause massive changes in cell cycle signaling and in cellular communication. mTOR is huge in longevity and in cancer progression via the dis-regulation of the p 53 gene.

Now you can see why when this pathway is activated many bad things can happen that are common to T1D, T2D, most cases of leptin resistance with elevations in HS CRP. These steps are stops on the way to either final pathway and we call them neolithic diseases. Yes.......you got an insight into Quantum Metabolic syndrome early.

We are not making it complicated......it is. This response is the proof of just how complicated it is.......and you need to have a lot of science background to understand this complexity......believe it or not I leave a lot of it out on purpose.......but not in this response.

so If I understand this stuff (biochemist I'm not). but NAD+/NADH when elevated and you have a faulty cytrochorme 1 means that your increased leakiness (regardless of reason for said leakiness) = greater mis-regulation of cellular signaling and directly impacts longevity and can stop cellular death (apoptosis).

however, NADP+/NADPH is the sweet spot for PPP; assuming you are in ketosis, have sufficient Pg and leverage the cold. This is system where autophage works its magic - and why you can reach a zero entropy state. niacin is another way to trick the body into accessing the PPP, however you've got to be aware that if you have a faulty cytochrome 1 (PCOS, Diabetes, Leptin resistance etc) it won't convert to NADP+, but NAD+ instead, which is why its better to get into the PPP leveraging ketosis, Pg and the cold.

### kathylu — 2013-03-13T04:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-80313>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=80138)
>
> Now you can see why when this pathway is activated many bad things can happen that are common to T1D, T2D, most cases of leptin resistance with elevations in HS CRP. These steps are stops on the way to either final pathway and we call them neolithic diseases. Yes.......you got an insight into Quantum Metabolic syndrome early.
>
> We are not making it complicated......it is. This response is the proof of just how complicated it is.......and you need to have a lot of science background to understand this complexity......believe it or not I leave a lot of it out on purpose.......but not in this response.

Hi Jack. I had to read this a few times to understand where you were going with your comment. I think you may have misinterpreted my intent. When I said "I think we often make things more complicated than they need to be", it was not a criticism of your logic, methods or intent. I do not feel "tortured" by the biochemistry...in fact, I love the complexity. It's just that I am always looking for a way to make things actionable. So, I have to ask myself if it is necessary for everyone to be able to know that the FADH2 production path in the Krebs cycle leads to more ROS and leakiness at cytochrome 1 and the hexose monophosphate shunt-PPP leads to the "magic" of the reductive path, more NADH which gives us higher glutathione levels and all the cellular benefits that brings? Maybe it is enough for most people to have a basic understanding of why ketotic, high fat diets can be corrective for a wide variety of conditions. That it can essentially "cure" type 2 diabetes, prevent cancer, slow or maybe even eliminate neurodegenerative disorders. I also love the connections you are drawing to quantum biology and the coherence of water, quantum tunneling and mis-folded proteins. But again, it always leads me to ask "What do I do now?", which if you think about it is the type of question I have always asked you in consults.

So, I guess I think the magic of being able to bring this critical knowledge to the largest numbers of people lies in simplifying and providing actionable techniques, not in the exploration of intricacies (no matter how much fun that is for geeky people like me). This is such a touchy opinion to express because I do not want to offend anyone, least of all, you. It's a laudable goal for the people who find these subjects fascinating (as I do), to work on acquiring the knowledge needed to grapple with the theoretical millieu, but I think most of us are simply crying out "What do I do now? How do I save myself, my family, my friends? How can I explain this to them in a way they will understand?"
Please understand, I am not asking you to "dumb down" anything you have been writing about. I personally like that you expect us to get with and stay with the program. I just feel that putting as much emphasis on the practical as on the theoretical can be helpful. Thus the genesis of my "reductive" statement!

so the more I think about this and PPP - I realize that I must be entering into to it on a more regular basis of recent? WHY? SLEEP has been my MOJO of recent - I'm so much about sleep - that it doesn't matter when I eat dinner - but within 45min of eating I'm falling asleep. could it be the HIGH PROTEIN 0 CARB dinner I'm eating. I'm falling asleep with the kids - waking up around 11pm, going right back to sleep and having the most amazing dreams, I'm waking up refreshed, I'm also not wanting to drink coffee either - What's with that? today I woke up 5mins before my alarm was supposed to go off.... just like that BAM! I'm awake... not hitting snooze, just rolling out of bed - ready to rock and roll.

not sure if its the magnetico sleep pad, the PPP or the combination of both -one things for sure is I'm loving the sleep - hoping it stays that way - as I move into the last portion of my cycle... come on sleep. ### Jonathin — 2013-03-13T19:12:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-80379>

kathylu

I think it is a real quandary regarding knowledge vs. action. It seems that action without knowledge eventually leads to a breakdown. If you don't know why you are doing something then you eventually will stop doing it or it mutates to being something different. After all the reason that we've all gone off into the ditch during out lives is because we were not really fully aware of our sense of well being and we did not have good intellectual knowledge to help get us on the right track. It was not necessarily because there were not caring people around us just that they were clueless about real health. On the other hand I think that people can start with some action plan but that needs to be followed up at some point with facts, context and explanations. I think we all have the impulse to help those around us but it becomes a problem if we don't approach it correctly.

( ) Yes, kathylu, niacin is a precursor to NAD+/NADH (cytochrome 1) and NADP+/NADPH (the magic of the PPP), which play essential metabolic roles in all living cells as I showed in EMF 4. Niacin cannot be directly converted to nicotinamide, but both compounds could be converted to NAD and NADP in vivo and this information is very valuable when you have liver leptin resistance, metabolic syndrome, or epilepsy. When cytochrome 1 has mis-folded electron transport proteins (quinolone issue of CoEnQ10) you need to bypass it 100% of the time by eating fats predominately because if you don't, your mitochondria begin to make massive ROS that overwhelms the cell lowers vitamin D and all hormones, causes pregnenolone steal syndrome, and eventually shortens its telomeres to cause cellular signaling problems that lead to either senesence or cancer. The reason why? NAD+ builds up in relation to NADH levels on a per unit basis. Increasing the NAD+ in comparison to NADH changes a most of the mechanistic downstream signalling in protein kinase B pathways of cells. Why is that big? The AKT pathway is also known as protein kinase B (PKB). It is a serine/threonine-specific protein kinase that plays a key role in multiple cellular processes such as **glucose metabolism, apoptosis, cell proliferation, transcription and cell migration.** Akt1 is involved in cellular survival pathways, by inhibiting apoptotic processes. Akt1 is also able to induce protein synthesis pathways, and is therefore a key signaling protein in the cellular pathways that lead to skeletal muscle hypertrophy, and general tissue growth. Since it can block apoptosis, and thereby promote cell survival, Akt1 has been implicated as a major factor in many types of cancer. Akt (now also called Akt1) was originally identified as the oncogene in the transforming retrovirus, AKT8. In a mouse which is null for Akt1 but normal for Akt2, glucose homeostasis is unperturbed, but the animals are smaller, consistent with a role for Akt1 in growth. In contrast, mice which do not have Akt2, but have normal Akt1, have mild growth deficiency and display a diabetic phenotype (insulin resistance/ T2D), again consistent with the idea that Akt2 is more specific for the insulin receptor signaling pathway. The role of Akt3 is less clear, though it appears to be predominantly expressed in the brain. It has been reported that mice lacking Akt3 have small brains. This is what causes brain shrinkage in all the neurodegenerative disorders that are linked to cytochrome 1 issues. PCOS is tied to this early one before the more chronic diseases come to the forefront.
> Akt2 is an important signaling molecule in the Insulin signaling pathway. It is required to induce glucose transport.
>
> The follow through? AKT1 is involved in the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway and other signaling pathways. These all cause massive changes in cell cycle signaling and in cellular communication. mTOR is huge in longevity and in cancer progression via the dis-regulation of the p 53 gene.
>
> Now you can see why when this pathway is activated many bad things can happen that are common to T1D, T2D, most cases of leptin resistance with elevations in HS CRP. These steps are stops on the way to either final pathway and we call them neolithic diseases. Yes.......you got an insight into Quantum Metabolic syndrome early.
>
> We are not making it complicated......it is. This response is the proof of just how complicated it is.......and you need to have a lot of science background to understand this complexity......believe it or not I leave a lot of it out on purpose.......but not in this response.

# Thread: Raw farmed fish bad idea?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-08T19:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79678>

So anyone I just wanted to see what would happen...I ate a bit of raw farmed Tilapia which had been frozen in my deep freeze. Are the advantages to raw food changed by freezing? Also I'm wondering if the temperature they were kept at is low enough to get rid of parasites.

I just ate a little bit...not much and than tried to feed the rest to my dog...he wouldn't do it. I did put some Bubbies pickle juice on it...maybe he didn't like that..or maybe its just cause he isn't used to raw food?

Note he does eat small pieces of Bubbies pickles I give him...maybe he just doesn't like the juice.

I'd like to have him on a raw food diet sometime if I can but he isn't on one now so maybe its just an unfamiliarity factor at play. I put some cilantro and parsley on it..perhaps that was part of it?

Any comments on the experiment and issues of raw food in general would be greatly appreciated. I read some articles recently on it..and while I know that wild caught would be preferred...I can't afford wild caught fish much at all...not with my money.

Anyone have any experience on eating raw food? Are there any foods that you can eat raw that aren't wild caught, pastured etc...I guess what I'm asking is can some non ideal food be more of a positive than a negative if prepared properly. Maybe not...maybe using farmed Tilapia was a bad idea. Tilapia is the right spelling isn't it?...I guess its not in the browser dictionary...it says its misspelled but no correction available...lame

### Hope — 2013-03-08T20:48:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79690>

Many people here eat some raw fish etc. Dr Kruse has said farmed fish is still better than most meats etc. FWIW. YMMV

And yes you spelled Tilapia right

I have fed some salmon (raw) to a friend's dog. Eat it right up. Was plain though, nothing more. Smoked too (Just very little). Again, all eaten right up. Maybe try without the sauce and herbs? Raw is GREAT for dogs for sure. Cats too. Have you heard of Pottenger's cats?

### diane — 2013-03-08T22:25:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79705>

My dogs eat a raw diet, but for some reason won't eat raw fish! Sometimes the skin, but not the meaty part. So I cook it a little and they will eat it right up.

### kathylu — 2013-03-08T23:31:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79721>

My local asian market has fresh farmed salmon (origin Canada) for $1.99 per pound on sale. Can't pass that up! I eat a lot of it raw, they clean and filet the fish for me (I keep the bones, skin and heads for other uses). I have sashimi and make ceviche the first night, maybe grill the other filet the next day. It's delicious. I guess wild is better, but this is good too.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T23:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79723>

We have eaten the farmed Scottish salmon raw sashimi style for a couple of years now. It's really fresh tasting and we have never gotten sick. That's got to be a good sign, right?

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-09T00:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79731>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=79690)
>
> Many people here eat some raw fish etc. Dr Kruse has said farmed fish is still better than most meats etc. FWIW. YMMV
>
> And yes you spelled Tilapia right
>
> I have fed some salmon (raw) to a friend's dog. Eat it right up. Was plain though, nothing more. Smoked too (Just very little). Again, all eaten right up. Maybe try without the sauce and herbs? Raw is GREAT for dogs for sure. Cats too. Have you heard of Pottenger's cats?

I know raw is good for dogs its actually ideal from what I've read which makes sense was just not sure why he seemed to avoid it. It being something he doesn't get normally and the things I had on it...were perhaps the issue. I'll try later with fish that has nothing on it to see if he goes after it.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-09T00:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79733>

Thanks for the comments all. Its good to know farmed isn't horrible. Getting used to eating raw fish...isn't going to be easy for me though. Part of its the coldness of it and part of it is the texture.

### diane — 2013-03-09T00:51:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79743>

I eat mine raw with pickled vinegar, or as Kathy said, as ceviche.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-09T01:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79751>

We buy sashimi grade salmon that is farmed and eat it raw, but I've never eaten 'regular' farmed fish raw.

### marthabob2u — 2013-03-09T04:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79773>

My Japanese neighbour told me that any fish that starts out in fresh water, then migrates to sea water, may pick up parasites and should be frozen before eating it raw just to be on the safe side. I LOVE raw fish. If anyone cooks my tuna I will get upset!

### Inger — 2013-03-09T09:06:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79788>

Raiken,
I have been eating all kinds of raw animal food for many years already. I would not eat the tilapia if there was some other option. I always try to check how are they being farmed and feed. It makes a difference, you see. I am very cautious about farmed fish from Asia and never buy those. I can get pollock here very cheap, Pollock from Alaska, wildcaught. It is prefrozen and I do not love the taste raw, but it makes me feel more awesome eaten raw so I usually do it in my mixer with spices, raw onion, raw ginger etc.. roll small balls or "burgers" and it turns out so delish!

Raiken, try to always look for wild caught first. Pre-frozen is okay. And look for where it comes from. Wild and farmed. Pick the cheapest and the best. You will have to spend some time searching, but I bet you will find something better than tilapia. Do not care if it is not your favorite tasting fish, you can spice it and make it tasty, I promise! Always go after how healthy something is and not if you like the taste.

I eat farmed Norwegian Salmon at times, I have been looking into their farming methodes and they are quite ok. Sure they get vaccinated and stuff and vegetable oils but still they get lots of shrimp scales and fish-chowder to feed too. So I sometimes eat it. But I try to really limit it and eat wild instead. Even if my taste buds like the salmon better...

If your intestines are not healthy it might be good to be cautious about parasites, IDK. I never had an issues, but my gut was not that damaged either. For sure a bit but not seriously. Then, if you eat some raw risky fish and then cheat all the time with crap, I guess that is a really bad Idea. I think when you have a very strict and healthy diet you can get away with some parasites too, but you have to forget about cheating. That is how I do, and it works so well!

# Thread: Optimal Spring Planting

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T22:49:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79708>

So those of you out there who are planning to do a garden, what are you going to plant? I'm looking for ideas on heirloom organic seeds. We have a rather large space to plant this year and it's time to get going on this project (at least it is here in So Cal). Also any good online resources anyone has to share for info or places to buy the seeds please let me know. Below is my starting list of what I may plant (not in any order). Most of these seeds are from Seeds of Change.

Beets - Golden, Candy stripe (haven't found this one yet)
Satsuki Lemon Cucumber
Lettuce - Butter Crunch Bibb, Lambs Breath (mache), red romaine, rouse de hiver
Rhubarb -
Onions - Yellow cippollini, Suskiora Sweet
Radish - French Breakfast
Swiss Chard
Okra - Star of David
Fennel - Zefa Fino
Melon - Sugar baby watermelon
Tomato - Yellow pear, pineapple, San Marzano (I think), plum and maybe brandywine and Arkansas or some others.

Of course the herb garden of thyme, cilantro, lemon basil, Korean licorice mint, chives, rosemary and parsely.

Let's share our ideas for which is the best company to buy from, the best gardening tips, and even what varieties you have been able to grow in your area.

I can't wait to start my spring planting this year, but I've been a little depressed too thinking that I have no reason to grow half the fruits/veggies we enjoyed in the past. I decided to focus on salad veggies (lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, etc) and brassicas with some zucchini and berries. I'll probably skip the melons and starchy veggies . . .

The melons for me was kind of a last minute throw in. During the season I think they will be a nice treat. Oh yes and we just planted two blueberry bushes but honestly I don't have a lot of hope for them since So Cal is not exactly blueberry territory (but the garden shop said these varieties will do well here). ### ashryn — 2013-03-09T03:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79771>

### marthabob2u — 2013-03-09T03:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79772>

Well whenever the snow leaves the garden at home in northern BC (which was June last year) I plan to plant spinach, swiss chard, lettuce, peas, carrots, kohlrabi, pole beans, radishes, a few cylindra beets, green and regular onions, garlic, and whatever else will fit. Homegrown produce is SO much better than store-bought! Success of crops in the north is weather dependent- sometimes we get lucky. And, lucky me, I get to trade excess veggies for fresh wild caught salmon and prawns.

In Mexico this winter I managed to grow tomatoes from seed- cherry tomatoes worked the best. Here i don't fight snow but there are a multitude of bugs, land crabs, leaf-cutter ants, iguanas, and the occasional chicken to fight off.

# Thread: Any suggestions what I need to do ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liv — 2013-03-08T23:07:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79715>

31 Oct 2012 Vit D25 40,4 ng/mL (ref 30-100) I have since then supplemented with 5000 IU every day. Today I got the results taken 5 March 2013 and it was Vit D25 31,3ng/nL(ref 30-100). How can I have so low D-vitamin level even if I have been supplementing with it ?

Other labs Taken 31 Oct 2012
Cholesterol 209mg/dl
Triglyceride 58mg/dl
HDL 61mg/dl
LDL 136 mg/dl
BUN 12mg/dl
Calcium 8.8mg/dl
Creat 0.7mg/dl
BUN/Creat 17.1
Glucose random 86mg/dl
Sodium 142mMol/L
Potassium 4.3mMol/L
Chloride 105mMol/L
CO2 26.6mMol/L
AGAP 14,7mMol/L
HgbA1c 5.5%

Labs taken 7 Dec 2012
Estradiol saliva <0.5 pg/ml low ,(postmenopausal optimal1,3-1.7)
Progestrone saliva 39 pg/ml (12-100 postmenopausal)
Ratiog/E2 78 low (optimal 100-500 )
Testorone saliva 52 pg/ml (16-55 age dependent)
DHEAS saliva 15.7ng/ml (2-23 age dependent)
Cortisol morning saliva 15.7 ng/ml (3.7-9.5)
TSH 1.280

Labs taken 21 Jan 2013
cardio CRP 0.35 mg/l (0.10-3.00)
DHEAS 210mcg/dl (15-170)

I am 52 years and have had big problems with fibroids since age 47. I have had several surgeries to take them away and it finally ended with a hysterectomy in Feb 2012 . Still have my ovaries but started to get menopausal problem in Oct 2012. My gyn MD put me on bio identical hormones now in Jan 2013. I take progestorone 100mg and have a weekly patch with estrogen. I am eating a LCHF low carb high fat diet since fall 2011 but now since a couple of months trying to adjust to epi paleo. I had an Oral Glucose test done in may 2012 it showed 166 mg/dl one hour and 169mg/dl two hours. I had pregnancy diabetes with my third child (have four children born 88,89,98 ,00)so I understand I am on my way to get diabetes if I dont try to be optimal. I have maybe 5-6 pounds extra weight and carries it around my belly. My waist has gone from 36 Inches to 32 inches the last year, but not so much change in my weight. I do understand CT should be my next step but it is so hard to do !
Any suggestions how to get optimal ?

### nonchalant — 2013-03-08T23:18:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79718>

Hi Liv, welcome!
Are you supplementing with D3 in gel capsules? Have you tried liquid vitamin D? I believe others have had success with vaginal application, where oral vitamin D wasn't helping because of leaky gut or other issues.

Your morning cortisol looks high. What is the reference range? You might want to reduce your exposure to light at night. Your cortisol may be stealing pregnenolone from your other steroid hormones, including vitamin D.

Please post ranges with your lab values so others can figure them out.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T23:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79724>

Agree w/all Nonchalant said. Also check your thyroid levels as those folks with low thyroid function have a tendency to have trouble in the CT tub due to cold intolerance. Just having your TSH tested in not enough. You need FT4 and FT3 w/Reverse T3 (if you can get it where you live).

### diane — 2013-03-09T00:48:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79741>

For Vitamin D - I was also supplementing and very low. I had to push way more than 5000 IU's to get mine moving up. I do between 10,000 and 20,000 per day, especially in the winter.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T01:32:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79746>

D ... get out in the sun. Barring that get in a tanning bed. That's what I'm doing since supplements didn't raise my levels at all.

I should just put this in my sig line ... my recommendation is to get TS Wiley's book, "Sex, lies & menopause" ... I've been on that protocol since July of 2011 and I feel GREAT. They can have my BHRT when they can pry it from my cold, dead fingers. I had a total hyst at age 51 and went rapidly downhill because of it. Oh ... a kilo's worth of fibroids to boot. I was anemic from the damned things bleeding me dry, literally.

That's all I've got. HTH.

### Liv — 2013-03-09T02:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79756>

Thanks . Cortisol saliva morning 7.6 ng/ml range 3.7-9.5. My supplementation of D3 is in liquid capsules. I just ordered glasses to block blue light from Amazone (the ones Misty has)looking forward to get them. Only other lab I have of thyroid is TSH 3rd gen 2.001mcIU/ml (0.550-4.780) but its quite old taken 5 Oct 2011. Its difficult to get labs because the MD sees in his opinion a healthy woman in front of him but I know my health is not optimal it can be better. I edited and put in the ranges of my lab in my first post.

Okay - so what is a good pick up line for a girl to use??? Clearly what I am doing isn't working!

### Inger — 2013-03-09T09:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pickup-line.6246/post-79789>

Huck.. I am coming too....!!!  (can I take my rotten meat too..?)

Caroline, my suggestions;
Be yourself 100% and enjoy the moment 100%.... be present and enjoy everything! IDK for sure because I am not a guy but I have the feeling guys like when a women do that... And you will like it too..
Never complain. Make sure you fly.... Stay open, embrace the word. Eat lots of raw. When I eat all raw I get a different glow! Do not ask me why... but once a guy come to me at the cafe bar and said.. wow.. you are so alive! And he looked at me so amazed. When I start to eat more cooked I loose that a little bit.
Always look into the eyes, but never stare. Always be polite. Eyes are amazing...

Do not complain to me if you cannot save yourself from guys running after you now.. you asked for it.. ha

### caroline — 2013-03-10T02:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pickup-line.6246/post-79894>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=79789)
>
> Huck.. I am coming too....!!!  (can I take my rotten meat too..?)
>
> Caroline, my suggestions;
> Be yourself 100% and enjoy the moment 100%.... be present and enjoy everything! IDK for sure because I am not a guy but I have the feeling guys like when a women do that... And you will like it too..
> Never complain. Make sure you fly.... Stay open, embrace the word. Eat lots of raw. When I eat all raw I get a different glow! Do not ask me why... but once a guy come to me at the cafe bar and said.. wow.. you are so alive! And he looked at me so amazed. When I start to eat more cooked I loose that a little bit.
> Always look into the eyes, but never stare. Always be polite. Eyes are amazing...
>
> Do not complain to me if you cannot save yourself from guys running after you now.. you asked for it.. ha

I completely and totally agree with what you are saying Inger! That is my life now - just naturally ...I am in awe of everything I see.

But I do think - I don't know how to say this properly - that it is scary or off putting to many people \[not in a bad way\] but they don't get it. It is like it is too strong or something.

I find now that I only seem to connect to like minded people who are inspired by life. There is a peacefullness and calmness that takes place ...whereas others seem to have no interest or life or joy about themselves.

I think it has to bubble up from the inside out and it just escapes naturally and some people don't know what to make of that....

I think it takes an extraordinary person to appreciate all of that.....

Don't know if I really said what I wanted too say......

ever since I got off sleeping pills about 1 and a half or 2 yrs ago I have taken several supps before bed. Melatonin I have used for years and then I added GABA and magnesium. sometimes I add valerian. Plus over the counter progest cream which my gyno says is placebo. This to get off Ambien. I do ok on this protocol....with some patchy nights. I get up 2-3 times per night to pee.....(not a good sign).
Please hack my sleep! Is it bad to take these supplements for extended time? Which ones should I quit? Or what labs should I have done?

### caroline — 2013-03-09T08:23:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-79782>

### nonchalant — 2013-03-09T16:00:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-79800>

I had purchased some melatonin just before I heard him say that, and they have been sitting, unopened, in the cabinet for a long time.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-79801>

Janagram do you know your DHEA levels as well? And have you done any grounding outside? My best sleep comes when I've been outside for large parts of the day - even before I knew the benefits of grounding.

### janagram — 2013-03-09T17:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-79803>

No, I don't know these levels. good place to start. thank you....I do not get enough sleep. ever. 5 hrs is about it.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-09T20:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-79826>

While Valerian is often considered a sedative, for some people it has the opposite effect, and works to stimulate rather than sedate. Other, more reliable sedative nervines include Skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora), California Poppy, Hops, and Pulsatilla. You can sometimes find them in combination. I have found herbal sedatives to be very useful for some people and situations.

### vkiernan — 2013-03-11T19:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-80071>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=79801)
>
> Janagram do you know your DHEA levels as well? And have you done any grounding outside? My best sleep comes when I've been outside for large parts of the day - even before I knew the benefits of grounding.

Me too, always sleep better after a day outside whether summer or winter. I have not read on my laptop in the evening for quite awhile now and my sleep is getting awesome. I don't have internet until my dh brings it home (that is usually late too) plus I live in the country where it has been tough to get internet in the first place. I only take my oral progesterone 1/2 my cycle and 1 mag malate now at bedtime and I actually sleep and could sleep some more if I had more time in the am. I am a true believer in the wrong light and emfs screwing us up.

Have you consulted Jack yet? With sleep like that, I think it would be a good investment. Without quality sleep, we are nothing. I really wonder how much autophagy you really have going on if you have to take all that stuff to keep your eyes closed at night? I feel for you and hope you find some good answers to get some real sleep soon.

### Hope — 2013-03-11T21:02:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-80080>

janagram, Do you ever CT in the evening? Even face? What's the temps in your room when you sleep? The cooler the better...

Would your MD be willing to give you 'real' PG (in pills) for your luteal phase at least? Do you notice your sleep getting worse with different phases in the cycle or is it always bad? Dr K has said oral PG pills may help with sleep, and it does wonders for me....of course that's my N=1 and YMMV but could be worth a try??

Have you tried drinking less starting in the very early evening to see if that helps with getting up for the bathroom at night? Or is it all the same?

Who gave you the sleeping pills? A sleep specialist? If so could you do a sleep study? I did one and learned I had pretty bad sleep apnea.

Have you always had sleep issues??

What about meditation, a calming routine at night? And no stress either in addition to darkness...if I get upset it's harder for me to relax well at night.

Good luck!

### Hope — 2013-03-11T22:49:13+0200

### fitness@home — 2013-03-12T14:23:38+0200

> ever since I got off sleeping pills about 1 and a half or 2 yrs ago I have taken several supps before bed. Melatonin I have used for years and then I added GABA and magnesium. sometimes I add valerian. Plus over the counter progest cream which my gyno says is placebo. This to get off Ambien. I do ok on this protocol....with some patchy nights. I get up 2-3 times per night to pee.....(not a good sign).
> Please hack my sleep! Is it bad to take these supplements for extended time? Which ones should I quit? Or what labs should I have done?

Janagram, I had a bad experience with Ambien about 3-4 years ago. My doctor prescribed it because my sleep was so horrible at that time. Even melatonin wasn't having the desired effect. I loved it at first because my sleep was so much better. At six months, I had to talk her into a refilling the prescription...was pretty dependent on it by that point.

The bad stuff started happening when I went through a horribly stressful period in my life. IBS was acting up and had meds for that and Xanax. The combination of stress with the meds was not good. I started having short-term memory issues - had stickies everywhere!. Also very foggy thinking, and even had a hard time driving in the morning (like driving from home to work and thinking, where did the time go in between the two points?). That was my wake up call.

I weaned off of them by breaking in half and then quarters. I was not happy to lose the "sleep" but it wasn't a natural sleep. Things are better now. I sleep well most of the time.

Glad you are off the Ambien.

### janagram — 2013-03-12T18:25:40+0200

ayyyyy......yi...well, it's a slippery slope with drugs. Been there....had to get off Xanax and Ambien, but sleep has never been as good. ( a period of extreme anxiety got me into it). I'm interested in the sleep rx. I'll try to order it.

### SarahS85 — 2020-02-03T04:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-285514>

Hi Guys,

Just wondering if anyone knows which is the safest melatonin supplement to take for children and adults?

### Jack Kruse — 2020-03-03T03:48:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-286985>

When you take supplemental melatonin more times than not Nature will punish your circadian mechanism around PER1/PER2 NAD+/SIRT1 levels to favor chronic mitochondrial colony failure. Acute melatonin administration in humans impairs glucose tolerance in both the morning and evening. When you take melatonin as a supplement long enough be ready for retinal thinning and redox collapse of your mitochondria in many organs.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4173928/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4173928/)

### Beristere — 2022-08-09T14:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-314937>

I also had trouble sleeping and took similar medications to help me fall asleep. But then I realized that giving them up would return sleep problems. It is also impossible to take such medicines constantly because, in addition to side effects, the effect of the medicines will weaken. Therefore, I gave up everything except the complex vitamins I buy in Online Pharmacy. What helped me was proper nutrition in combination with a vitamin complex, and sports also play a very important role. About 3 hours before bedtime, I go outside and work out on the horizontal bar for up to an hour and a half, then I come home, eat, wash, and I’m so tired that I can fall asleep much easier, and my sleep has become more qualitative.

### JanSz — 2022-08-09T15:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-since-i-got-off-sleeping-pills.6249/post-314939>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=286985)
>
> When you take supplemental melatonin more times than not Nature will punish your circadian mechanism around PER1/PER2 NAD+/SIRT1 levels to favor chronic mitochondrial colony failure. Acute melatonin administration in humans impairs glucose tolerance in both the morning and evening. When you take melatonin as a supplement long enough be ready for retinal thinning and redox collapse of your mitochondria in many organs.
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4173928/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4173928/)

I suspect that this study is suspected.
They used 5 mg melatonin.
And that is almost nothing when compared to 180mg melatonin.

in Sweden...away from a city...no doubt? which must be a good idea...

# Thread: Shocked at Trifield Meter readings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-09T20:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79829>

I just got my Trifield meter yesterday and have been playing since!! I am really surprised by some of the readings though . . . the kids' ipods barely register (unless charging) and my cell phone is the same, even DH's iphone. Most of the appliances register much, much higher than the handheld electronics. I never would have expected that.

Someone mentioned their ceiling fan had a high reading, but ours shows nothing. CFL bulbs are horrible, but halogen and LED give nothing. This is so interesting. I can't stop playing! Anyone else find something that really shocked them?? Problem is now that the kids saw that their ipods aren't as bad as I've been telling them I'm going to have trouble convincing them to give them up. I was hoping for the opposite to happen.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T20:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79833>

Check the iPods when they're connected to wifi and d/l something. Same with the cell phones. It's when they're connecting that I think the real issues begin. I could be wrong. Someone will correct me if I am.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-09T20:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79834>

The Trifield meter is a good starting point. However, no one meter will measure all EMF.

Your answer for the kids is that Wifi on iPads, etc is RF (radio frequency), and the Trifield meter does only a marginal job of detecting RF.

Radio frequency or “RF” refers to high frequency fields and microwaves emitted by microwave ovens, cell towers, cell phones, cordless phones, TV and radio broadcast towers, Wi-Fi, wireless computer devices, and other electronics. The electric and magnetic parts of RF fields are not separated, so you only need one meter to detect the RF field. RF is usually measured in units of microwatts per centimeter squared (μW/cmÂ²).

[http://emfcenter.com/metrsale.htm](http://emfcenter.com/metrsale.htm)

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-09T21:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79837>

Ditto....unfortunately the wifi isn't going to be measured well with the Trifield. We just got our taken out of the house all together. It makes me feel like I'm going in the right direction.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-09T21:18:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79838>

What type of energy are you measuring? Motorized devices and CRT monitors put out huge magnetic fields. Wireless routers, cell phones and cordless house phones definitely put out microwaves between 900MHZ - 2.4+ GHZ. Turn on the phones and they put out more. I have been in home offices that have large amounts of RF. I would take the [Concerto](http://coherentresources.com/emf_protector.php) with me to minimize adverse health effects when working on computers there. The owner's body was in constant pain and her joints were disintegrating literally. This was over 5 yrs ago when I knew less. My measuring device is not a tri-field meter but only detects microwaves. I don't remember the name at this time. Microwaves are arguably the worst emf unless you include the terahertz airport scanners!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-09T21:28:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79843>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=79838)
>
> Microwaves are arguably the worst emf unless you include the terahertz airport scanners!

I've been wondering about exposure to the old fashioned metal detectors. Any harm in that? Probably?

### kathylu — 2013-03-09T21:31:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79845>

Me too. I was using an electric blanket to pre-warm my bed, then I turned it off before getting in. Thought that solved the problem. No! The meter literally showed more voltage when the blanket was off than when it was on! Now I am unplugging it...problem solved and I still have a warm bed.

### Martin — 2013-03-10T00:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79863>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79843)
>
> I've been wondering about exposure to the old fashioned metal detectors. Any harm in that? Probably?

I doubt it, it's a measuring device rather than a transmitting device.

### Inger — 2013-03-10T07:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79905>

Read the instructions that came with the meter to more effective use of it, it will help.
The iPods and cellphones gives out pulsed EMFs, you need to hold the phone to the left side of the meter, very close, and wait. Then you will see how it send the pulsed EMF all the time. Tell your kids the pulsed EMFs are really bad for us as they do not exist in nature at all. Ipod has a place I think on the upper right side where it has way stronger radiation, you must move the meter over it to detect it. You need to touch the screen and have it on, too.

The TriField meter is not too sensitive meter but good enough to get a picture.
Put to cellphones close, maybe a feet away.. and call the other, with the meter in between. You will get shocked at how strong and far it reacts!

### Hope — 2013-03-15T17:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80679>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79843)
>
> I've been wondering about exposure to the old fashioned metal detectors. Any harm in that? Probably?

I am sure they are not great. But still better than the new stuff. I refuse them every single time. I Just hope they don't phase out the old kind some day..

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80680>

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-15T18:09:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80682>

I've turned the wifi off on my phone I won't turn it back on...

### Rachelle — 2013-03-15T18:25:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80685>

I'm so screwed. I live by a transformer grid yard and there is nothing in between my apartment and it. I took out my wifi and I am not watching TV, bought a corded phone and a Himalayan salt lamp, but I know in my heart I am not safe. I have lived here now for a 1 1/2 years and didn't realize there was a transformer grid yard right across from me. What is up with that? I was oblivious to it. I was so worried about everything else going on I missed it.

I really just want to pack up very little, move to an ocean spot and pitch a tent. That is the bohemian in me but there is also the conservative person I grew up to be gnawing in my head.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-15T18:56:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80688>

So my homeopath said it's actually the electricity in my home that is actually guarding me from the strong fields from the grid yard. And to stay off the street--never hang around here. That until I can move.

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-15T22:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80700>

Sniffed out the house with the Tri-Field meter and, thank goodness, we seem to be doing very well. The bedrooms were clear, but now I can prove to my husband that I am not a "Nervous Nellie" for having wanted nothing plugged in near the beds and having all audio/video equipment plugged into grounded power strips that are turned off when not in use. I am also very glad that I have always made people sit FAR away from the TV, computers, and EdenPure heaters in my home. I did find a mysterious spot in the kitchen wall near where my daughter sits at the kitchen table. I bet it is some sort of fault in the wiring. We will be moving her spot at the table while awaiting an electrician. Our old desktop computer is horrible and so is our multi-function printer. I am glad we barely ever go in the office. I will be keeping them off unless in use. The laptop does not seem to be much of a problem at the distance I sit from it when it is unplugged from its AC adapter. Plugged into the AC adapter seems to make it just a bit worse. The signal from the cell tower 1380 meters away is not perceptible through the windows on the side of the house facing it--I know this is not the correct meter accurately measuring RF, but I am glad that whatever that tower is putting out is imperceptible to the Tri-Field. Working up the courage to go stand by the smart meter and see what it registers on the Tri-Field (again, I realize it is not an accurate meter for RF).

### kathylu — 2013-03-16T04:28:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80721>

I felt better almost immediately when I had the SmartMeter removed from my house. See my journal for the story.

I took the TriField meter out to my car today. I have an Acura 3.2 TL ...oh my god...it's bad! Electric and magnetic measurements literally off the meter, a little better in the back seat. I don't know what to do about it...gotta get reading about mitigation in cars! And I really don't want to think about what I'll find when I measure the Toyota Prius tomorrow (electric hybrid car)...maybe get a bicycle?

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T03:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80928>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=80782)
>
> I'd love some input about how to ground this set-up...my poor snake has been well insulated for years and I'd love to get him grounded!

Endless, how about attaching a wire to a flat piece of smooth metal and just set it in the aquarium? If he likes it, he will lay on it.
Otherwise, you could attach a grounding wire to a piece of metal screen or hardware cloth in the bottom, and put rock and such on top.
Either way, you might want to have some wood in there so he can get away from the effect. I think at first the grounding can be a strong sensation, and he might want a break.

# Thread: Ozonated Water

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4

### IvanDrago — 2013-03-09T23:11:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ozonated-water.6258/post-79850>

The only water I can afford is ozonated. Is ozonated water safe to drink?

### Inger — 2013-03-10T08:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ozonated-water.6258/post-79915>

Ivan you could maybe make it alive again by adding some raw dried or fresh seaweed, or wild edible herbs like nettles or such in it (those are all for free, just try pick them in a clean place away from the city at best), and put it in a glass jug in the sun (in cloudy weather too) outside to get UV rays....? And you could earth it? Put it out, let the sun beams in, the greens.. and then a earthing rod too (dig it deep into the earth) in the jug. How does that sounds?
I bet you will have magic water....
