### JanSz — 2013-03-13T18:22:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-fglucose-goes-up.6309/post-80371>

> [pmgamer18 said:](/goto/post?id=0)
>
> Read this link in this it talks about dumping your sodium.
> [http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal\_Glands.html](http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal_Glands.html)

This section caught my eye.

> Actions of Cortisol
> Blood Sugar
>
> Cortisol is essential for maintaining blood sugar (glucose) levels in the proper balance. A drop in blood sugar triggers the adrenals to make more cortisol. The cortisol increases blood sugar levels by converting fats and proteins into energy in a process called gluconeogenesis. In this energy production process first fats are broken down into fatty acids and proteins into peptides and then these are converted into the needed blood glucose. This process is vital to keeping the blood glucose levels relatively constant throughout the day. Your body depends upon glucose as its most consistent form of energy. Cortisol works in tandem with insulin from the pancreas to provide adequate glucose to the cells where it is burned for energy. Cortisol ensures adequate levels of glucose in the blood while insulin unlocks the cell membranes to let glucose into the cells. When your body is under stress from any source, there are more demands placed upon its various tissues and organs, requiring more available glucose to fuel more energy production in the cell.

I think I am rather successful in using MLM pregnenolone.
Raises my preg
Raises my prog
Raises my cortisol
TCortisol, serum 8AM(18.1)(6.2-19.4)ug/dL

wait
my fasting glucose is going up 93, 106, 115 WTF

but Spectracell is saying that my Glucose-Insulin interaction is high into good area of their chart =54 (>38)
could use tiny bit more chromium

And I do not remember when last time I ate any grains or potatoes.
I got rid of 1500mg/day Metformin after my 23andMe told me that may Metformin benefits may be less that those of average person.

I am back on GlucoCare, looking for Bitter Melon and other similar stuff.
But possibly I am making too much cortisol because I take too much pregnenolone (scratching head)???

ideas???
...................

# Thread: Am I doing it right?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arclite — 2013-03-13T19:28:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80386>

Greetings fellow travellers. This is my first post here as you can very well see and I will make it brief. I have begun a CT regimen as of 2 days ago. I lie on my back with two 10lb bags of ice on my stomach and chest whilst wearing a compression shirt. This I do for an entire hour. periodically i remove the shirt but put it back on as it becomes agonizing within a matter of minutes. I am 250 lbs so i don't shiver or really feel that cold at all during these exercises. Questions: Is it of utmost importance for skin temperature to remain in the 50-55 F range, or is the coldest I can tolerate the most effective CT? Can I get my skin so cold during this activity that I'm in danger of causing tissue damage? If skin temp drops below 50 F should I remove the ice? Is a skin thermometer a neccesity for this activity. Does CT reduce adiposity more effectively in areas which recieve direct contact with cold or is its effect global? I would greatly appreciate your insights on these issues.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-13T20:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80397>

Welcome, Arclite!
Be careful with ice. Yes, you can cause tissue damage. Watch for white skin. The compression shirt would help prevent frostbite.
The safest way is to put the ice into water first.

The safety of ice depends a lot on the condition you are in when you start. How long you have avoided grain oils, etc.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/)

Direct contact with cold is best, but there is also a global effect.

### Arclite — 2013-03-13T23:20:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80417>

Thanks man. I have read and reread the easy start guide but their are things which I am uncertain about. My skin turns pink in reaction to the icing so I do believe I am safe in that regard. I understand 55 f is the level at which surface cold receptors are activated, and skin temps above this are unproductive to CT. It seems to me however an hour of contact between 20 pounds of ice and my abdomen would result in a surface skin temp >50 F. I think though that this is where the thermogenesis portion comes into play and that this mechanism would act as a buffer, heating the tissue in response to the the prolonged cold to maintain temps in the 55-50 range.
As I am new to this practice and presumably poorly adapted to cold, should I take it easy? I skipped face dunks and my first session of icing I could endure an entire hour without much difficulty. That's why I think I might be doing something wrong. I expected wailing and gnashing of teeth but got only modest discomfort. Maybe I'm already a bit cold adapted as I have just experienced a frigid south Dakota winter with inconsistent and inefficient heating, or maybe I'm just fat.

### caroline — 2013-03-13T23:49:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80419>

As Nonchalant asked - how is your diet? diet is extremely important.....are you doing epipaleo? Do you have access to a bathtub?

### Dali Dula — 2013-03-13T23:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80420>

Welcome Arclite. You should read some of the posts in Cold Thermogenesis. There is a lot of experience there. A few things in response : Dr K's protocol does not involve immersion in temps below 55. There are many of us who have gone well below this. Go slow, be safe. When using ice and compression gear I don't think tissue damage should be an issue. You want pink skin not white. You should do face dunks as they help rewire your cold pathways. Face dunking actually makes you body feel better when immersing. I also found the icing easy in the beginning. You should move on to full immersion. That's where it's happening.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-14T00:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80423>

Heh, wailing and gnashing of teeth is what most of us expected. But many of us have found that our bodies adjust rapidly.
I don't actually worry about skin temps. I just get into the bath when it is a certain temperature, and just time myself. Occasionally I check the water temp when I get out.

Perhaps you are well adapted. I understand not everyone has a bath available, either.
Just be careful about timing, a few of us have actually damaged skin, and that takes a while to recover from.
I will often have issues with parts of the bottom of my feet after a very cold bath, though the arch will be just fine.

With icing you just have to be a little more careful, imo.

### Arclite — 2013-03-14T01:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80429>

I've been eating epipaleo for a little over three weeks now. Not optimal yet but light years ahead of the SAD I have eaten for the last quarter century! I should eat more seafood but its expensive and shitty in my part of the country. I'm supplementing with krill oil now. Totally keto, almost no carbs. no lights after sunset, monster breakfast. There is such a wealth of information available on this site Its easy to miss some things. I have a lot of catching up to do. I have lost 15 lbs to date, but i've only had 2 CT sessions yet. I didn't even realize CT was part of the protocol until just a couple days ago. I have a tub I can use but it pretty cramped for a big dude. oh well I'm gonna give it a shot.

### caroline — 2013-03-14T02:19:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80447>

Sounds like you are getting on top of all this ... there is so much here to get thru and then invariably - you do have to reread to get more from it the second and third time...so don't get discouraged! I would re think the Krill oil ...JK's not a fan. Have you considered sardines and canned oysters and canned salmon and mackerel etc. etc. They are still way better than GF Beef. Have you tried a MHS yet? that is so inexpensive to make and super good for you.
And lamb's liver and heart or GF beef heart and make steak tartare. There is a lot you can do that is quite inexpensive.

Before you know it - you will be fitting in your tub - no problem!!!

### caroline — 2013-03-14T02:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80449>

Don't forget to take pictures so you have before and afters - really great thing to do to see your progress!

### Arclite — 2013-03-14T18:34:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80511>

Did 50 min in the cold tub at 51 F. I felt like I could have gone almost indefinitely , if only I were not in such an uncomfortable position. Tub slowly drains too so I had to figure out the amount of water to leave on to maintain the correct height. Wow! I've lost 5 lbs in the last 3 days! My face is totally leaning out. No ones noticed yet, but in my experience that usually doesn't come until about another ten lbs. I listened to a podcast with Dr. K in which he recommended krill oil, some fella named Abel who wondered if he could apply the 80/20 principle to CT and Kruse shut him down. Optimal or GTFO!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-14T18:43:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80514>

Arclite, sounds like you're a natural. Did you shiver long after getting out? I usually shiver for about an hour.

### Arclite — 2013-03-14T19:03:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80519>

Not too much actually. When I came out of the water I felt a bit uncoordinated and had bizarre electrical sensations (like hitting your funny bone) running up my arms. The shivering didn't kick in until I went to bed, about a half hour later. Had to get up and grab another blanket. I don't dread the CT anymore. I don't miss lights after sunset. What I miss is being able to lay in bed for 45 min after I wake-up. Aha! I'll cook breakfast the night before and eat it in bed!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-14T19:41:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80526>

During the CT bath, and afterwards, I often get a strange numbness in one of my toes that I broke long ago. And my ankles can be a little stiff. I find it interesting that we can walk after an ice bath. It's almost like our feet are designed for it.

### diane — 2013-03-14T21:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80532>

> [Arclite said:](/goto/post?id=80511)
>
> Optimal or GTFO!

Should be a t-shirt. LOL.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-14T21:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80535>

Congrats on your 5#! That's great success. Probably the CT is helping a lot especially w/those temps and times. Good for you and keep it up!

### caroline — 2013-03-15T01:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-80601>

You are doing great!!

### Arclite — 2013-03-24T01:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-81587>

My Brother has finally got fed up with me turning his house into my CT station. I have an old drum case and if I sit cross legged I can submerge myself in it. Yesterday evening I did my first CT in the drum case and it was excruciating. for the first time since my very first session I had to get out for a bit because of the pain. I broke my thermometer so I couldn't check the temps. Today I check the tap water and its only 40 F at my place as opposed to 51 F at my brothers place. add 8lbs of ice and I must have been in the mid 30's. I am not acclimated to temps that low yet and had extreme pain in all sensitive areas that were submerged. The pain subsided in about 15 min but it was intense!

### endless — 2013-03-27T02:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-doing-it-right.6310/post-81928>

> During the CT bath, and afterwards, I often get a strange numbness in one of my toes that I broke long ago. And my ankles can be a little stiff. I find it interesting that we can walk after an ice bath. It's almost like our feet are designed for it.

I notice that the balls of my feet feel thicker after CT...I feel like I'm walking uphill!

# Thread: Holy Tri-field readings batman!!!

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-14T00:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80425>

so the trifield meter came today ...turned it on.. low and behold the kids room is off the charts - literally w/the exception of the wall they share with the bathroom... so tonight is furnature re-aranging time and it looks like emf paint is the 1st house hold project we're going to do...

havoc's bed lays along the highest measurements... changing that tonight...

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-14T00:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80427>

Yikes, I tremble to think what my house is like. Still saving up for the meters. An engineer friend told me that the Tri-field is only useful for detecting magnetic fields, like from an electric motor or home wiring, but that it is next to useless for quantifying radiofrequency waves. The description from the manufacturer regarding the Tri-Field seems to say the same thing. For an RF meter, he said you have to spend $300 to get an accurate amateur version. Given all of my neighbors' WiFi, DECT phones, and smart meters, I think I will need both types of meters. We can't decide whether to spend money on shielding or the meters first. I have my daughter's bed as far from outlets as possible, but I also have it away from the outside wall because there is a cell tower 1380 meters away from that side of the house. When I mapped out all of the cell towers in our area, we really are about as far away from them as you can be without worrying that someone is going to put one up next door next week--and we live in a rural area! They are literally in a circle around our house with the 1380 meter-away one being the closest.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-14T00:55:25+0200

>
> havoc's bed lays along the highest measurements... changing that tonight...

At least smart meters weren't invented at that time.

### kathylu — 2013-03-14T02:10:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80443>

Glad you got one! Don't forget to check the ceilings...I had hot wires running through mine. And the electric range...off the meter, even when I am not using it!

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-14T03:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80464>

Just got my Trifield meter. Very interesting. Focused on my computer desk area since I work there. Currently have 3 monitors (2 LCD and 1 LED). The oldest LCD (manufacture date 2006) was high off the charts .... even when both cables were unplugged ... guess it really retains a charge. That monitor was retired today. The LED monitor barely registered, and the other LCD monitor was mid-range.

But the most interesting discovery was with the mouse. .... it registered very high, and I use the mouse alot more than my keyboard. Guess that explains why I have more inflammation in my right hand than left. Apparently all mice manufactured currently have either optical or laser sensors; so that seems to explain the high EMF of a mouse.

Solution for that seems difficult. Either find an old 2 button mouse w/ no optical, or find a good way to ground myself. The latter seems more feasible ... anxiously awaiting the EMF webinar so I can learn more about safe and effective grounding.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-14T14:28:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80484>

besides the microwave setting the meter off the charts - the dryer, blender and vacuum do as well... at least now DH is ready to invest in some mitigation solutions for the kids...

our bedroom is really good - except for the alarm clocks - the cool thing is the tri-field meter brought everything home for dh....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-14T14:52:40+0200

when you test you get smarter.........

### Terminator — 2013-03-14T15:48:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holy-tri-field-readings-batman.6313/post-80491>

I have been turning off circuit breakers in the house for same reasons. My kids, 7, 10 & 13 years old can tell the difference add the younger ones ask me to turn power off because they feel and sleep better.

Lately i have been thinking of how to mass inform people of EMF's so that we can fight back the stupid power (power lines, smart meters), cell phone and electronics companies. Not many believe guy like me even if i explain in detail EMF's huge negative health impact but if they hear from a doctor such as Dr. Kruse, they may take warning more seriously.

### Joann — 2013-03-14T01:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-link-anyone-heard-of-this.6314/post-80430>

Hi all,

I have this pendant I bought at my chiropractor years ago. Supposedly is protection from EMF.

"The Q-Link reinforces our natural bioenergy by activating an omnidirectional, protective field extending around the body. It acts like a tuning fork, enhancing the body's innate and creative intelligence to align with the basic bioenergetic rhythms found everywhere in nature...."

Too good to be true?
company name is Clarus.....says developed with scientists from UC Stanford. shown to reduce EMF levels up to 90%

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-14T01:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-link-anyone-heard-of-this.6314/post-80432>

I have one and wear it during my 13-hour shifts at the hospital (a sea of EMF)--just in case it works. I have a hearth arrhythmia that is triggered by EMF, especially DECT phones. I have noticed a slight difference when wearing the Q-link vs. not wearing it. When I wear it my heart arrhythmia kicks in between 11 a.m. and 1 p.m. When I do not wear it, my arrhythmia seems to kick in by about 9 a.m to 10 a.m. I get to work at 6:30 a.m. However, I cannot rule out some sort of placebo effect or spurious variable, and I have never done any tests with meters or such. It does seem to improve my balance a bit, but again that could be placebo. A man I know swears that when he does not wear his Q-link 24-7 he gets headaches and loss of balance from the smart meter on his home--that is why I bought one. Basically, inside the Q-link is a copper coil resonator, which physics says does amplify fields. As to whether or not it can amplify our biofield, when touching us, in a way that truly protects us from man-made EMF . . . I am still not sure.

# Thread: My Journey, Here I go.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-here-i-go.6316/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### njdx1205 — 2013-03-14T02:13:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-here-i-go.6316/post-80444>

Well tomorrow will be one full week for leptin reset for me. I think I will write in my journal once a week to give me a since of acomplishment. Here's my week...........

BREAKFAST
chicken breast w/coconut oil on it (3 makes 64 gr. protien)
or
sausage & eggs & bacon (about 63 gr. protein)

\*\*I only get meats that are already cooked, because I 4 kids and if its not easy I won't make it through. I try to get good Quality ones.

LUNCH
Did not have a need to eat it all week, but i did one time for me and my husbands date lunch. I had a veggie stir fry w/shrimp, only ate 1/3 . The rest of the week I had no desire to eat lunch.

DINNER
Very Lite all week, I feel so in control all day after my big breakfst.
spinach salad W/coconut oil and carb free dressing
Salmon or chicken or steak

oh and 2 cups of coffee w/non dairy power cream. (2tsp-2carbs)

I think I am eating about 10 gr. carbs per day

For the first 5 day, about 45 mins after eating i got hot flashes for about 3 hour. About day 2 I started to feel very tired all day, and very sleepy after dinner. But today in the afternoon I got a big Burst of Energy out of no where. I've been extremely Thristy all week ,which is not like me. Oh and 2 days ago i sleep alot deeper than I normal do, I noticed it first thing.

I did not cheat one time this week and i am so amazed at that. The big Breakfast has given me my willpower back, it feels so good to have a sense of control again...... Its been so long

well i will have to post the rest tomorrow, kids calling......

### caroline — 2013-03-14T02:44:52+0200

### njdx1205 — 2013-03-21T23:01:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-here-i-go.6316/post-81363>

Well today make 2 weeks on leptin reset. This week was fairly easy. I had a few craving for sweets, probly because I was around cake alot this week, One of them I baked. I am proud of myself for resisting ALOT this week. I had 2 morning where I just could not force myself to finish BAB. And on those morning I had to eat dinner early.

breakfast
eggs
bacon & sausage

Lunch
none

dinner
spinach salad
steak or fish
except for 2 days , I ate a bag of pork rinds for dinner besause i was in a hurry.

Weightloss - A STINKING 2 lbs .but weightloss is weightloss, so I will take!

Things I will do differently this week:

Get a full 8hrs of sleep
take my supplements everyday

things i notice

some days i'm very tired and sleepy
somedays i get very energetic
I am sleeping better and deeper
I HATE the BAB , but it gives me control so I love it at the same time.

Staring wt- 156 lbs. Current wt- 148 lbs. Goal wt-124 lbs

# Thread: Suramin to reverse autism?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suramin-to-reverse-autism.6318/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-03-14T11:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suramin-to-reverse-autism.6318/post-80481>

Suramin is an inhibitor of purinergic signalling that has been used to treat African sleeping sickness since shortly after it was first synthesised in 1916.
The drug targets a cell messaging system that produces a metabolic response to stress.
According to a new theory, autism is strongly linked to this pathway, known as purinergic signalling.
Scientists in the U.S. found that the drug corrected 17 types of abnormality linked to autism in genetically modified mice, including social behaviour problems.

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/a...ssful-clinical-trials-mice.html#ixzz2NVMTD3tF](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2293113/New-drug-help-reverse-autism-tested-children-time-successful-clinical-trials-mice.html#ixzz2NVMTD3tF)

# Thread: strength gains in two weeks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jenaf — 2013-03-14T18:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/post-80516>

Hi everyone,

Just wondering if anyone, since cutting back EMF exposure and drinking 3-4 litres of spring water a day has noticed any differences? I've been using epi-paleo, mmm oysters!!, for the last year to try to recover from massive adrenal fatigue (some CT but I'm going to hit it up more soon), and in the last month introduced grounding in the ocean 2-3 times a week. But in the last two weeks I haven't grounded at all, but I've made a huge effort to drink 2-4 L of local non flouridated spring water everyday and shutting off the breaker to my bedroom, washer/dryer, microwave and turning off WiFi when not using it. (and FINALLY getting a full nights sleep!!!)

and in the last two weeks I've gained 6-7 lbs but my clothes are loose (i can comfortably wear a pair of pants I haven't worn in about 8 months - so dropped close to a dress size). Today I also decided, hmmm I'll try to do a turkish get up with my 16kilo kettlebell - haven't done that in almost a year, and the last time i did one was about 3 months ago with a 12k bell, and that was difficult. Bam - easy up, both sides.

Anyone else notice any differences like that?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-15T00:23:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/post-80581>

Many more gains will come your way if you keep it up........

### jenaf — 2013-03-15T03:46:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/post-80629>

Thanks Dr Kruse, it feels amazing to FINALLy be making some progress!!!

### caroline — 2013-03-15T04:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/post-80636>

It seems like you are doing great...good on you. I am feeling quite a bit stronger too and definitely clothes fit different. Everything was getting way to big!
Progress seems to come in fits and starts and sometimes you don't even notice and then one day you go WOW when did all that happen?

This journey has so many benefits and rewards - please keep us up to date on how you are doing - it helps everyone.

### Inger — 2013-03-15T07:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-gains-in-two-weeks.6324/post-80642>

Jenaf, that sounds just amazing, how you are doing..!!

IDK what I weigh because I do not weigh but I get comments at work like; "you are so thin!" "You look so strong!" and believe me I am not thin at all.. but I do like how my body look now and I do zero exercise, it is winter and I really live a sedentary lifestyle very different to the summer.. I eat huge amounts of fat too. I do tried to make a pullup just for fun a week a go or so and it was easy peasy.. I need to go and try now if I can make some more..
I do feel that the drinking water and grounding thing makes some difference..

### Rachelle — 2013-03-14T21:30:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-able-to-use-magnesium-now.6327/post-80543>

Not sure what happened but I mentioned before that when I used mag malate or theronate my limbs got got, extremely, heavy and I was fatigued the next day. I just tried it again 3 weeks ago and it did the same, and the only thing I changed was to stop drinking fluoridated water. Have only been drinking spring water now for 2 weeks and decided to try both mag malate and theronate again for the last few days and have no heavy limbs or fatigue at all.

I am very tuned into my body and have been for many years. I normally add one protocol at a time for this very reason. Been sleepiing much better also and I now believe sleep more important than food.

Anyone know what is up with that?

# Thread: Fasting or not?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-14T21:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80549>

I find this so frustrating. Every doctor I deal with has a different opinion on whether or not I should be fasting for these tests, soooo I've decided I trust all you experts much more than I do the 'so-called' experts

Do you fast for the following:

Thyroid panels
Sex hormones (PG, DHEA, E2, T, etc)
hsCRP

I'm also getting a cholesterol panel and glucose, which I always fast for and a vitamin D that I've never fasted for.

### caroline — 2013-03-15T00:51:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80588>

I don't think so - but I am certainly no expert. I just learned that you can be fasted for too long before Glucose testing. I usually eat dinner around 4:00 or 4:30 pm .... so now I really have to think about having some food around 6 or 7pm or even as late as 8pm. I usually get tests after seeing my Doc and if he is running behind I can be over fasted.

### caroline — 2013-03-15T00:52:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80589>

I usually call the lab myself now and ask about requirements ....

### diane — 2013-03-15T01:00:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80592>

When I had mine done they didn't require fasting for thyroid and sex hormones. I did for the hs crp, but that's because I got it done with all my other labs.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-15T02:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80612>

Answer seems easy ... if all labs are drawn at the same time, and even one lab requires fasting (i.e. glucose), then you fast.

I always fast and always test at the same time in the morning .... With consistent fasting, and draws at the same time, I can rule out one variable.

### caroline — 2013-03-15T04:56:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80638>

>
> Answer seems easy ... if all labs are drawn at the same time, and even one lab requires fasting (i.e. glucose), then you fast.
>
> I always fast and always test at the same time in the morning .... With consistent fasting, and draws at the same time, I can rule out one variable.

Great advice - thanks....

### Destiny — 2013-03-15T12:44:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80657>

My GP doesn't mind to draw my cholesterol when I am not fasting. He says that actually you see what is happening in your day to day that way.

That makes a sense to me.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-15T12:50:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-or-not.6328/post-80658>

The best way to test cholesterol is fasting but after 32 oz of water........todays blog shows you why. This is why so many people have elevated LDL on testing.......and guess who tells you not to drink before the test? The guys who make statins.......just another truth bomb.

I'm trying to be smarter about it this time  Sooo if I can have some done in the morning and some in the afternoon (preferably in the afternoon so I don't have to mess up my BAB), that's what I'm hoping to do!

I'm on 4 grains of NDT (was diagnosed a few years ago after over 10 years of trying to GET diagnosed) and will be getting sex hormones tested this next month. I'm 100 pounds overweight despite my best efforts and have the energy of a slug. A 32 year old, female, dead slug.

And I have NO idea what it means to have zero DHEA. That seems ... bad. You need a really good doctor, stat!! I suspect your life may depend on it.

### Slone07 — 2013-03-15T22:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-dhea-hashis-thyroid-oh-my.6330/post-80701>

LOL - Well, that doesn't sound hopeful, y'all.

My doc started me on DHEA, some cortisol lowering/calming supplements, vitamin D3 (yes, he wants it up, up, up) and wants to try some anti-virals to try to modulate my autoimmune issues (I'm still looking into this, but what he told me about it sounded promising). I took a DHEA on the way home from his office and within ten minutes, the mind-numbing headache I've had for the past couple of weeks vanished. It didn't wander off...it just disappeared. Made the hour long drive back home much more pleasant. It's currently a few hours later and I still don't have a headache.

As for D3, that one's nothing new to me. I've always been low, trying to get into the upper half of the range. Hey, at least this time it wasn't 13 with the same range...  I've been out of it for quite a while and between that and the apathy that takes over when I feel bad, I just never ordered more. I do live in the deep south, so plenty of sunlight here, but I do need to try to get outside more. I tend to avoid it, because I burn so easily (and I hate sunscreen). It's also exhausting, for some reason.

I'm on Thyroid-S from Thailand. I've been self-treating since my old endocrinologist retired a few years ago and left me with her replacement who was appalled I was on "that old stuff" (Armour) and wanted me immediately on Synthroid. Been looking for a non-TSH doctor and three days ago...I finally found one. It's been at least 3 years, though, of slowly increasing my dose based on symptoms. It kept going up and up and 4 grains was kind of a point that I wasn't willing to go past on my own. Instinctually, I knew that there was way more wrong with me than just my thyroid and Hashi's. Anyway, the new doc recommended not going up to 4.5 grains given that he's starting me on the other stuff and said that my body may begin dealing with the thyroid hormone a little better soon.

I'm getting thyroid (and antibodies) and cortisol retested in 6-8 weeks and DHEA and other hormones in 3 months after DHEA has had some time to work.

I knew there was a reason I felt like I was 95. It's because I am! On the inside, anyway. LOL

ETA: I must be feeling a little better...I used two emoticons.

### caroline — 2013-03-16T00:17:22+0200

# Thread: Just a few question from a beginner
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-a-few-question-from-a-beginner.6331/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### njdx1205 — 2013-03-14T22:59:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-a-few-question-from-a-beginner.6331/post-80555>

will i need to continue with 60 gr. protein breakfast after i see all leptin sensitve signs? or is this just to regain sensitivity?

### caroline — 2013-03-14T23:31:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-a-few-question-from-a-beginner.6331/post-80559>

> [njdx1205 said:](/goto/post?id=80555)
>
> will i need to continue with 60 gr. protein breakfast after i see all leptin sensitve signs? or is this just to regain sensitivity?

Once you begin to see signs - have a think about how you are doing with 50 gr. protein breakfast......do you enjoy it and is it keeping you sated until dinner?
Think about adding in more fat and cutting protein a little. For some of us that is the way to go because it was very hard to stuff down all that protein!

Everyone's journey is very different ....don't be in a hurry but do enjoy the ride!

### LRichard12 — 2013-05-31T12:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-a-few-question-from-a-beginner.6331/post-87177>

What if I'm finding myself still hungry after eating a breakfast of 70 grams protein and 66 grams of fat?

A 25 yo coworker told me yesterday he just had brain surgery because of a brain tumor... he is vegetarian too... and a other young lady coworker told me she has gotten really bad panic attacs and have to take heavy medication to be able to work at all.. they got so much worse after around Christmas this year (when almost all got a smart meter installed).. ### TheKid — 2013-03-15T10:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/may-be-harder-than-we-think-to-get-away-from-emf.6332/post-80644>

Yeah, I think the race is on. Now you see why Jack ain't waiting around to hear more bad news. I can see it in myself.

The problem is, I think in most cases EMF's effects aren't as noticeable. You don't realize it until it's too late. And the biggest concern with EMF is cancer. You don't feel Oncogenesis. By the time you feel the cancer, you're in deep trouble.

So people who downplay EMF do so, I believe, based on a lack of clear symptoms. They don't realized that when you start getting palpitations, GI manifestations, migraines, cancer ... you're already pretty far down the road.

Day after day I read about people with cancer diagnoses that seemed to pop out of thin air. Young people. Guess what, it should take you years of diet-driven metabolic derangement to come up with a cancer diagnosis. And before that, you would see a helluva lot of other medical conditions that should be warning signs. So when I see kids showing up with Leukemia and other cancers and other exotic diseases, I'm not thinking diet. I'm thinking acute toxin exposure and I'm thinking emf.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-15T12:02:39+0200

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-15T16:33:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/may-be-harder-than-we-think-to-get-away-from-emf.6332/post-80668>

All I can say is that damn Tri-field meter really opened DH's and my eyes - we now have an urgency to fix the kids rooms. one things for sure - running the washer dryer will happen when the kids are OUTSIDE not inside...

My son gave me a laptop 18 months ago, and we still don't own a flatscreen tv. I wonder if this is why I had no trouble at all when I started taking Lugol's? But dh is a whole other story. He spent a decade flying interstate every week for business, and when he wasn't on a plane he was in the middle of an emf firestorm in his office. We are hardwiring his office this afternoon, and grounding him to a copper waterpipe outside his office window.

# Thread: Female Hormones on Day 20 -- please help/hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-15T22:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80697>

Ugh. So got my labs back from Day 20....in a cycle lasting ONLY 23 days

Would love to hear someone's input.....it all looks pretty bad to me.

SHGB 80, range is 17-124 nmol/L - should be 25 or below, UGH. I am messed up....

Progesterone 6.8 ng/ml luteal phase range is 2.6-21.5 - LOW
Estradiol 51 pg/ml; range is 56-214 in luteal phase = LOW
Testosterone FREE 1.2 pg/ml, range is 0.1 - 6.4 = LOW
Testosterone Total - 19 ng/dl, range is 2-45 ng/dl = LOW

Well no wonder I am not getting pregnant.....everything is LOW. I can't do anything at all, it appears....ugh.

Definitely discouraged. I try so hard but I am just getting..low.

I will have more labs drawn next week.....

### diane — 2013-03-15T23:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80704>

Hope, it also helps to calculate your PG/E2 ratio. Here's the calculator that Shijin posted in another thread.

[http://instacalc.com/6162](http://instacalc.com/6162)

When you have test results, at least you can see where the issues are - then you have a place to start. It was much harder for me before I knew what to test, and then fumbling around not understanding why the diet changes weren't working.

Do you have an anti-aging doctor - or one who can prescribe BHRT? That's a good place to start!

### Hope — 2013-03-15T23:50:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80705>

Hi Diane,

thanks so much! I did calculate it, but I don't need it to tell me my ratio would be bad given to start with (it's 115 should be 300?).

My MD does prescribe BHRT....though I only take DHEA; PG in the luteal phase and just started Pregnenolone as well, based on my equally low numbers from before....ugh. Just discouraging...... Can't wait for my other labs to come in and have another edu consult.

SHBG is up too...ugh.

Anyway....thanks.

### kathylu — 2013-03-16T05:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80732>

Hi Hope. Don't get discouraged...I managed to get pregnant in my 40's and have a healthy baby! One thing that helped my hormone levels tremendously was acupuncture. I went to a traditional Chinese medical doctor...she did acupuncture with me twice per week and gave me herbs (vitex and some weird Chinese stuff...lol). Four months later, I was pregnant. It would have been a lot cheaper if I had skipped IVF and went with Chinese medicine first. I also took DHEA and estradiol (total disclosure).

### Hope — 2013-03-16T17:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80776>

Hi Kathy,

thanks so much for sharing, that is awesome! I did acupuncture from June through September and then November through mid-February. But, even a natural IUI didn't work (DH has some morphology/quality issues which I had hoped we might override?)...just this February. It makes sense since my hormones etc are still bad...but we decided to do one natural IUI timed as well as possible in February as I had been feeling a LOT better but that didn't work either....and now I know why. I may have felt better but my body is still not ready, it seems.  I did just start herbs so we'll see if that helps.

It's SO awesome that the acupuncture helped your hormones. In my case, it did nothing - or maybe it helped them staying bad, not getting worse. But certainly no improvement, yet. At least the female hormones. My FSH went higher in this process - actually doubled from 10 to 20. So I couldn't even do IVF (though we wouldn't have anyway, too expensive and I wouldn't want all those drugs in my body and force my body it may not want to anyway; plus I am super sensitive with drugs)

A wonderful acupuncturist I've talked to, she thinks I have some kind of internal blockage, or lack of openness....which I need to really think about....I do know I've been kind of stressed/unhappy living where we do (outside DC) as I hate it here.....though I am working on accepting it as much as possible/making the best (and having Dr K and all of you helps) of it all. I totally believe EMF has been affecting me here and it's been great getting a real understanding of it all now....

I just recently started DHEA....so we'll see if that makes a difference. But I have tried a lot - even wheatgrass....but some days, like now, it's harder to stay positive...I've been trying to get well enough for so long...ugh.

Anyway THANK YOU so much for sharing your story...and giving me hope!

### Eve — 2013-03-16T18:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80781>

> SHGB 80, range is 17-124 nmol/L - should be 25 or below, UGH. I am messed up....

Where did you get the recommendation that your SHBG should be >25?

### Hope — 2013-03-16T19:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80794>

> [Eve said:](/goto/post?id=80781)
>
> Where did you get the recommendation that your SHBG should be >25?

Dr K has mentioned it should be below 25.....

### kathylu — 2013-03-17T03:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-80823>

Hope, btw I live right outside of DC too. I can certainly commiserate on the stress filled environment. Good things too, of course. I love the museums and the cultural life here.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-20T01:51:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-81141>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=80776)
>
> I did acupuncture from June through September and then November through mid-February.
>
> It's SO awesome that the acupuncture helped your hormones. In my case, it did nothing - or maybe it helped them staying bad, not getting worse. But certainly no improvement, yet. At least the female hormones. My FSH went higher in this process - actually doubled from 10 to 20.

I think I would suggest a different acupuncturist. Did this particular one prescribe any herbal formulas for you to take in conjunction with the acupuncture? The right formula will be effective without acupuncture.

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=80776)
>
> A wonderful acupuncturist I've talked to, she thinks I have some kind of internal blockage, or lack of openness....which I need to really think about....

Have you been incorporating grounding/earthing into your weekly routine? Is there a place you can go where you can fully and safely connect with the earth? In my personal experience, as well as working with women clients, I have found the most effective way to find the key to the deepest internal dialogue is to be in touch with the ground. This will sound very "woo," but if you can find a place of power (waterfalls, certain rocks, mountains, hills, etc.) where you can lie on the ground, or be in the water, and where you can just start talking to yourself, out loud if you can, and start asking about what it is that is going on, and what are you feeling, and what might be holding you back, you might be highly surprised at what happens.

### Hope — 2013-03-20T02:52:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/female-hormones-on-day-20-please-help-hack.6348/post-81149>

Hi Chrysanthemama,

thanks so much for your input. LOVE your suggestions....Yes, I'm on a customized program of expensive herbs since October.....only took a break for two weeks in late Feb. I just was tired of it all. But am on them again now.

I am switching acupuncturists, I think...though for the third time. When I used to live in PA, I had one acupuncturist for several years and it was amazing with her.....but here (in DC) I can't seem to find someone I really click with (which is important to me)....so I have thoroughly questioned if acupuncture is really worth it, for me, right now.

As to grounding...not as much as I'd like. It's still quite cold here, though I try to spend an hour outside usually. Days like today when it's in the 50s, I do ground but only the feet. When it gets warmer, I can lay in the grass and in the park and have done that for years (without knowing about grounding/earthing). Incidentally, when I listen to the depth of my heart, what I long for is to spend like two weeks by the ocean...and walk in it for an hour every day. Just me, no one else there...just be with myself, connect with my heart. Even without kids around, my life is so busy and I run around so much, even on vacation, that I just wanna go, be with myself and stay put. LOL. Mountains and hills are harder to come by around here, but there are rivers and a lake, and I hope to go kayaking when the season starts.

And yes, I know I have inner doubts/fears....I am so 'old' for getting pregnant....but mostly I think I don't believe I ever CAN get pregnant, because it has always seemed like such an elusive dream (I foolishly decided at 36 to get my amalgam fillings out, at the time thinking I'd have "enough time" to get pregnant, UGH) and I've always pushed it into the future. I guess I've always longed for that so much - I never really had family besides my mom and dad, as their parents were all gone by the time I came along. I know my BRAIN is totally messing me up, my thinking.....even Dr Kruse has said so, in a consult, though that was about another thing. And it's true. I think I'm finally starting to see that more clearly.....after all, I am doing SO SO MUCH that 'looks right' (including EpiP, minding light, CT, oxytocin) but I am not getting the results I should be getting (well, in some ways, anyway, there are things that are OK like I have low HS CRP,good HDL, Vit D etc etc but my hormones, those are never great.)

Anyway, I'm babbling and must stop. Thanks again. Love your blog BTW. What a dream of a place to live...

# Thread: Satellite coaxial wire for grounding?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-16T05:20:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/post-80730>

I can't keep track of these grounding threads (wink).

Today at work I had a discussion about shielded coax cable. Coaxial cable for satellite tv has double shielding around a center copper wire core. In some of these threads there has been mentioned the fact a long copper wire is subject to EMF effects. Maybe not so with the tv coax wire since it has the extra shielding possibly acting as a faraday cage around the copper core. There is foam between the shielding and the copper wire. The center wire IS copper but it isn't very large. Anyone think the coax cable is a handy wire to get a ground wire from outside through the window? The insulation can be peeled back to insert the copper wire into the ground outside or connect it to a rod already in the ground. It is not unusual to have old coax laying around.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T04:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/post-80932>

Interesting, Glen. I don't have any, but it would be an inexpensive solution. Would it be too thick to go under the window?

### Optimalbound — 2013-03-18T12:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/post-80946>

Sounds like a good idea, Glen. I would make sure that the coax shield is connected to ground at one (and only one) end for it to work best.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T14:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/post-80956>

Normally, the center copper wire is used for signaling and the braid is used for ground. If you are going to use the center conductor as a ground to connect to your sheet, you probably also need to connect one end of the braided shield to ground; otherwise, the braid could build up a charge from external emf. Probably not a big deal, but worth the extra couple of minutes to ground the braid (or foil).

### av8r — 2013-03-18T16:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/satellite-coaxial-wire-for-grounding.6353/post-80972>

Adding a Ferrite bead to the grounding wire should help suppress any ingress... best to put the bead closes to your grounding strap or bed sheet.
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferrite\_bead](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferrite_bead)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Stressed out by 'stuff'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-16T22:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80811>

Just came across this great article.

[http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/10/o...ith-less-a-lot-less.html?pagewanted=all&\_r=3&](http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/10/opinion/sunday/living-with-less-a-lot-less.html?pagewanted=all&_r=3&)

I resonated particularly with this:

In a study published last year titled â€œLife at Home in the Twenty-First Century,â€￾ researchers at U.C.L.A. observed 32 middle-class Los Angeles families and found that all of the mothersâ€™ stress hormones spiked during the time they spent dealing with their belongings. Seventy-five percent of the families involved in the study couldnâ€™t park their cars in their garages because they were too jammed with things.

Although I've never lived in more than 1,200 sq ft of space, I do feel every day like we have too much 'stuff'. I've been getting rid of 'stuff' little by little but I sure would love to get rid of most of my stuff. I find books are the hardest....ugh.

I do remember when we moved, and the movers had packed up everything except what we would take, I felt so much lighter and....free. Maybe that's cause our ancestors had so little too

### caroline — 2013-03-17T00:18:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80813>

I think that is so true Hope....I hate all the "stuff"

When we moved to Australia we sent everything by a slow route so we wouldn't be in a position to just take any place we found because we had to. We found our place a few days after we got here and lived without our stuff foor 4 months.

We bought a bed and a patio set and a TV and a couple of plates and glasses etc and that is how we lived. It was heaven!

### Hope — 2013-03-17T01:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80821>

THat's so cool....where did you move from? I'd love to hear!

We went without our stuff for three months, too, though in our case it was in storage all that time....and I didn't miss anything except for my favorite kitchen knife. I would live that way again, too.....put all our stuff in storage, live in a furnished room till we figure out the next step. It's just so so nice to have much less stuff around.

### diane — 2013-03-17T03:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80824>

6 years ago we had to move out of our condo into a temporary condo. Insurance put all of our stuff into storage and we just took our day to day stuff. Then when we moved back (which was 9 months later) we cleaned everything out as we realized over that time we didn't need as much stuff. Now, 6 years later, stuff has accumulated again. Blech. Makes me want to purge it!

### caroline — 2013-03-17T06:51:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80844>

Yep - same here.....a bunch more stuff has accumulated...... I think I would love to live in a caravan and just travel around. This country is great for that.

Hope - we moved here from Canada.

### caroline — 2013-03-17T06:53:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80845>

I understand that you can get a caravan and part of the roof can slide away and you are sleeping under the stars! How amazing would that be???

### Inger — 2013-03-17T07:12:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stressed-out-by-stuff.6363/post-80846>

Oh yes.. and then one could just drive to sleep in a place far from all Wifi's..yay!

You all made me want to start sorting out and purging stuff.. I like to have only few things and the things I do have must be stuff of high value to me. When I go shopping I often say to myself.. hmmm, that I do not need, and that not either, also not that.. wow is it amazing to need so little. I have made it to a habit to really think twice if I need something. That has resulted in me buying almost nothing the last years. IDK but it feels so blessed.  Like i have really started to value stuff I have instead!

### prAna303 — 2013-03-17T08:15:09+0200

My mother - who grew up in Germany in WWII and its aftermath, who had nothing forever and used to have to eat potato skins with candle fat (there was nothing else for some time in the deepest years of the war, in the winter etc) - has an especially hard time giving things up, though I admire her incredibly for her courage and going through WW II hell, losing her mother at age 7 and many uncles in the war....and I think it must stem partly from that. She has clothes that are 30 years old. Still in great shape. Out of style, but in great shape. She just can't let them go....

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-17T04:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/same-and-homocysteine.6366/post-80828>

Can anyone point me to info on this website about SAMe? A young man asked me about it while I was shopping at the HFS, but I really wasn't familiar with it. We read the product label which said it is a neurotransmitter and important for joints & cartilage. Is SAMe something I make sufficiently on an epi-paleo diet or does one of the Rx's suggest supplementation? My eyebrow was raised when I researched this a bit later and found homocysteine was a potential toxic byproduct of SAMe supplementation so it is important to get adequate B12 and B6 to convert homocysteine back to an intermediate.

I was curious why someone in the prime of life would be wanting SAMe so I asked him if he was getting enough sleep or if he worked nights. He replied that he halfway got enough sleep and he would be working nights soon. I pointed out that sorta getting enough sleep was not enough sleep.

After losing 60 pounds via epi-paleo I had creaky joints, but it has improved some, and wondered if SAMe would be helpful or should I not bother?

# Thread: How Mitochondria Make ATP - Tossing Out The Old Theory
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-mitochondria-make-atp-tossing-out-the-old-theory.6367/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bogman — 2013-03-17T07:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-mitochondria-make-atp-tossing-out-the-old-theory.6367/post-80848>

.
I haven't seen anything on here about the updated research on how Mitochondria make ATP. Seems like Jack is still using the old method that was taught a long time ago? I'm still reading through all the info being new here.... In the old days; high school biology taught the Sodium Pump within cells - which is now very outdated theory.

**Looking at the amazing work of Dr Ling:** Oxidative Phosphorylation and Mitochondrial Physiology: A Critical Review of Chemiosmotic Theory, and Reinterpretation by the Association-Induction Hypothesis

The concept that the basic unit of all life, the cell, is a membrane-enclosed soup of (free) water, (free) K+ (and native) proteins is called the membrane theory. A careful examination of past records shows that this theory has no author in the true sense of the word. Rather, it grew mostly out of some mistaken ideas made by Theodor Schwann in his Cell Theory.

Revolutionary findings cast a new and different light on the subject of ATP synthesis. The emphasis is shifted from ionic or "osmotic" gradients, central to the membrane-pump theory, to ion adsorption on proteins, central to the Al hypothesis. XCH2CHEOOH

After the major discovery of ATP and its pivotal role in cell metabolism and function, ATP was believed to contain a high-energy bond in which an unusually large amount of potential energy was stored. Since the 1950s it has become clear that this concept is not correct. The enthalpy of hydrolysis of the ATP phosphate group is not unusually high; the free energy of hydrolysis of ATP is largely due to the liberation of H+ in an environment maintained at physiological pH as well as to other extraneous factors.

ATP acts as a cardinal adsorbent

This all ties into how, who and what cells function. The liquid inside a cell is structured. The old theory was it was some where between water & ice - only some what structured. Digging deep into the work of Dr Ling is amazing - starting in the late 1950's. Interesting work - I've found it fun to follow the extensive research.

### freesia — 2013-03-17T09:34:21+0200

[http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-7-quantum-prometheus/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-7-quantum-prometheus/)

Try this one

PS Agreed here, Ling's work is fascinating!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-17T13:50:20+0200

# Thread: How Water's Electrical Charge Effects Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-waters-electrical-charge-effects-health.6368/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bogman — 2013-03-17T08:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-waters-electrical-charge-effects-health.6368/post-80851>

.
How fluids like water, juice, soda, and so on; effect your health is based on it's electrical charge. This can be measured with an ORP Meter - which measures the very slight voltage in the H2O. When we do this we are actually measuring the presence of oxidizing or reducing agents by their specific electrical charge(millivolts), thus Oxidation Reduction Potential. High pH (alkaline) water has more "reducing" agents (negative \[-\]ORP) and low pH (acidic) water has more oxidizing agents (positive \[+\]ORP).

reducing agent is an "antioxidant" - negative \[-\] ORP. That's very helpful in gaining better health.

oxidizing agents (positive \[+\] ) is what steals electrons causing us to "rust" on the inside - not helpful in gaining better health.

The ORP of most tap water in the USA is between +200 to +600mv and so is an oxidizing agent. Wonder why you can drink water til your bladder bursts & you're still thirty?! Yep - wrong electrical charge. Even more shocking - the average fancy pants bottled water in your grocery store is over +600mv. Here you are working out and yep drinking bottled water - about as helpful as smoking while riding a bike!

What to do? Find a very high quality mineral water with a pH over 8 & TDS (total disolved solids) over 2,200. Hard to find in your local stores. Most people turn to San Pellegrino Water - If I remember correctly it's barely 400 TDS and just under 8 pH. I personally use Mineral Water from Mineral Wells, Texas - a company with a funny name: Crazy Water. They have a mineral water called **No. 4** \- not for the faint of heart
**No. 4** is over 2,600 TDS and a pH over 8.2 - which makes â€“ORP and so is a reducing agent or "antioxidant".

www. famouswater .com

Here's one more item to super charge your water. I've just started tinkering with this so I"m still figuring it out. It's called the "STIR WAND"
It looks like an adult toy in your nightstand. Rather an odd shape for something you put in your water glass.

**STIR WAND:**

THE MISSING LINKS TO SOLVING THE MASSIVE, CRITICAL HEALTH PROBLEM OF DEHYDRATION AND THE SOLUTION TO BRING HYDRATION, OXYGENATION AND CELLULAR DETOXIFICATION TO SUSTAINABLE THERAPEUTIC LEVELS

In the late 1990′s, Melonie Montgomery, PhD, of Fenestra Research in Las Vegas, NV, revealed her seminal work that discovered the correct chemical balance of water

Fenestra Research studied nearly 50,000 people in a 10 year period; all drinking enough water, and found that a whopping 94% of those tested for hydration were still significantly dehydrated !!! WHAT ?!?! I was blown away. Well now what?

Itâ€™s crystal clear that whatâ€™s significant in hydration is the chemical balance of water - THE STIR WAND.....

[http://stirwands.com/?page\_id=1127](http://stirwands.com/?page_id=1127)

That was when my blood would form a bubble on my finger and seemed stickier. It seems that the flouride free spring water has entered my blood supply.

### Dextery — 2013-03-18T12:50:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flouride-free-spring-water-and-blood-viscosity.6372/post-80947>

This is getting interesting to me. [http://www.herbprof.com/prof\_notes.htm](http://www.herbprof.com/prof_notes.htm) Be sure to follow the link as there is more about the blue zone of Seventh Day Adventists around Loma Linda Hospital in California

_Reducing Heart Attack Risk

What does this mean for those concerned with minimizing heart attack risk? According to the researchers, “…this is the first study to record the association between high water intake and reduced risk of coronary heart disease.” As Dr. Chan had stated in the press release and during conferences on her findings, “…by drinking more plain water, healthy people—without any history of heart disease, stroke, or diabetes—reduced their risk of dying from a heart attack by half or more. This is as much or more than if they had adopted any other well-known preventive measure, including stopping smoking and lowering cholesterol levels, increasing exercise or maintaining ideal weight.” Dr. Chan continues, “…the degree of benefit from drinking plain water surpasses that of drinking a moderate amount of alcohol intake and aspirin with none of the adverse side effects \[social or physiological\]. Because drinking more plain water is a simple lifestyle change that anybody can do, this simple practice has the potential of saving tens of thousands of lives each year with minimal cost.”_

_It is imperative to distinguish pure water from other beverages. As Dr. Chan puts it, “Neither total fluid intake, nor intake of other fluids combined showed this reduced risk. Instead, for women, high intake (5 or more glasses a day) of other fluids showed a greatly increased risk of coronary heart disease.” To emphasize a critical point, Dr. Chan adds, “People need to be made aware that there is a difference, at least for heart health, whether they get their fluids from **plain water** or from sodas.”_

Is this nature's coumidin or warfarin for protection against another heart attack?

Why have we not heard about the role of water in heart health? Maybe because big Pharma cannot make any money prescribing water.

Nothing was said specifically about flouride free water but I'll take Dr K's rec and stick with my flouride free spring water.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-water-and-your-health-interesting.6374/post-80878>

.
I tried to post this a few minutes ago but I think it got lost in space? I don't see it posted. Sorry if it did and I'm reposting - didn't mean to. Crazy computers have a mind of their own sometimes.....

.
I hope this helps to clarify how important elecrical charge is in the body. Even in college level chemistry classes they don't mention pH is tied to electrical potential in our body. More importantly how the change in pH alters electrical potential causing disease or healing to take place. I could be wrong here so feel free to correct something I missed

Crazy Water company makes a **No. 4 mineral water** that is the strongest mineral water on the market. More than 2,600 TDS and a proper pH level. A pH of 7.00 has a zero electrical charge - only with a pH lower @ 7.00 can water conduct electricity. 7.1 to 14.9 ==== Distilled water is 7.00 which means it can NOT carry electrical charge. www. famouswater dot com for more info on Crazy Water - Mineral Water.

The electrical potential is measured in water with an ORP meter which measures the very slight voltage in the H2O. When we do this we are actually measuring the presence of oxidizing or reducing agents by their specific electrical charge, thus Oxidation Reduction Potential. High pH (alkaline) water has more "reducing" agents (negative \[-\]ORP) and low pH (acidic) water has more oxidizing agents (positive \[+\]ORP). measured in Millivolts (mv)

An oxidizing agent, it is expressed in +ORP \\ this steals electrons causing free radical which is not helpful to healing.

A reducing agent is expressed in terms of â€“ORP \\ this is termed an "anti-oxident" that counteracts free radicals, good for healing.

Therefore, acidic foods and drinks have a pH above 7.00 / ranging from 0.00 to 7.00 which steals your electrons causing "DIS" ease.
The human body functions best with a pH hovering around 7.35 to 7.45 or in electrical terms, -20 mV to -40 mV. Some teenagers can hover between -40 mV to -60 mV they are bouncing all over the place with energy. If your pH dips below optimal that means your electrical charge drops below healthy levels - Cancer can only develop in a certain pH range & therefore, your electrical charge will drop below -5 mV all the way to +50 mV. In order to heal from disease/injury you need to become more basic with your pH to bring the electrical charge back to healthly levels (-20 mV to -40 mV) That means you need to be over -60 mV before you can heal. Without getting to in depth.... Cancer takes 1 mol of glucose to make only 2 ATP - a healthy cell takes 1 mol of glucose to make 38 ATP. That means the Cancer Cell needs to make 19 x's more Glucose to survive. It's in panic mode looking for any extra Glucose. How does it fix this problem? Cancer Cells figured out how to produce insulin receptors - sort of akin to fly paper to attract more flies (glucose). Pretty clever. That's a whole other topic that gets deep fast.

The real kicker is EMF, stress, life ect... all tend to steal your electrons causing your pH to be altered which leads to a loss in your electrical potential from healthy -20 to -40 / to unhealthy levels or +ORP. A tiny change in pH from a healthy level of 7.65 & your health tanks causing your pH to fall to 7.35, sounds like a small charge right? However, pH is measured in a Logarithmic Scale. Therefore, 7.35 is 1,000 times more acidic than 7.65, a much larger change than we realize.

This is way you can drink tap water til your bladder will explode and you're still dehydrated.......... drum roll....

The ORP of most tap water in the USA is between +200 to +600mv and so is an oxidizing agent !!!

So much for regular water being healthy - furthermore, bottled fancy pants water is worse. Most bottled water is over +500 mV!
If you're on the treadmill running your buns off and drinking bottled water it's like riding a bike & smoking at the same time.

Drinking mineral water from Mineral Wells, TX - aka Crazy Water - will help keep you hydrated & in proper pH range. Mineral water will help your body to conduct it's electrical current. Equals healthy.

Why do Vitamin C IV's help ? Ask this question to a Naturopath or Medical Doctor sometime. They won't know the answer!
The answer is: you're giving electrons. Electrons = healthy you. A healthy cell with good electrical charge burns ATP better. Your engine is running like a race car & not like a lawn mower engine (unhealthy). Did you know a fever is the body's amazing ability to turn up the furnance to create more energy (more electricity) It's the body saying help! & going into healing mode. Cold blooded animals, a lizard, when they get injuried seek a warm place (laying in the sun) to increase their body's temp - therefore, increasing their healing ability. What happens now when you get sick? DRUGS ! Masks the body's own ability to heal. You only get worse.
Try explaining that to your family doctor - they'll think you need to be in a padded room lol

Before the Great Flood during Noah's time on Earth - people did in fact live over the age of 200. Can't be done? The men of Monte Picchu are fathering children at 110 years old! No Viagra - they can get a healthy erection at 110 years old  WOW right! Our ultra-modern lifestyle of today is killing us. Time to go back to living in nature, naked, free from modern life.

Cooked food causes the body to produce more white blood cells. The body sees food as an evader causing our pH to get worse. Even pain causes the body to freak out and cause a loss of electrons. Why does lidocaine help? yep! Gives you more electrons and therefore, less pain.

Anywho - hope helps. I know there's so much to understand and learn.

Voltage = pH

one last point. Did you know you can take a voltmeter & measure the mV of your teeth? if a tooth is dead or has a metal filling it'll show up as +ORP. A dentist uses this same device (called Cavitat or Oral Potential Meter) the dentist can see early decay in your tooth. Who knew.... Sad that dental schools don't teach the importance of this concept - only thing they teach is reading x is bad and reading y is good. ugh

# Thread: Best days for hormone panel testing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HeidiHen — 2013-03-17T20:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80880>

I'm hoping someone can help me out.

1). I believe I read that day 21 of my cycle is the best day to take the female hormone panel blood test. But day 21 won't be for another two+ weeks. And quite honestly, I don't know if I can mentally wait that long.  Is there another alternate day that is OK for female hormone testing?

2). If I'm also taking a salivary melatonin and ASI test, is it best to do all testing on the same day? Or doesn't that really matter?

3). Does time of month / day of my cycle matter for a thyroid panel, melatonin, or ASI?

Thanks so much for your help!

### diane — 2013-03-17T21:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80885>

I was told day 21 for female hormones by my doc. But don't believe it matters for thyroid or ASI/melatonin.

Be patient - it's better to have good information than information you can't use. In the grand scheme of things, 2 weeks isn't that long.  I waited for my day 21, then realized we hadn't included estrogen and progesterone! So had to wait for another doctor's appointment then for day 21 to come around again. LOL. So I understand the impatience.

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-17T22:04:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80889>

Thanks, Diane! You're right.... I need to be more patient and be certain to have the correct information. I'm just so frustrated with my lack of progress despite 3 + years of trying. I've been trying to do it all on my own, with my own research and self-diagnosis. It's high time I do it right and wait for the correct day to get the best and most accurate results I can. I'm hopeful that I will finally have answers; I just want the answers NOW!

..... deeply breathing..... sigh.....

### diane — 2013-03-17T22:08:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80891>

I hear you Heidi. Mine has been one step forward, 2 steps back. But at least there's forward movement!

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-17T22:14:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80893>

LOL.... I hope you meant TWO forward and ONE back! I'd really be happy with that, too.... I'd just like to see SOME sort of forward movement!

### diane — 2013-03-17T22:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80895>

Haha - yes, good point!! LOL!!

Definitely 2 steps forward one back. And maybe not always back so much as at a standstill. And sometimes the steps forward are smaller than I would like. That's just my impatience talking.

I have the same issues sometimes. But then realize the small things - like the fact that drinking a cup of coffee one day doesn't set me back for 3 days of headaches (I was so coffee addicted). Or that I don't feel like eating as much anymore, even though I sometimes still have sugar cravings. Or that going on a hike doesn't wipe me out for the rest of the day. Sometimes I have to sit back and recognize the progress that I've made.

For me the biggest thing was getting my sleep on track and limiting artificial light. That's been huge for me. I had days where I couldn't get out of bed - and it's been months since I've had one of those. Even though I still have things I'm working on, that's been huge!

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-17T22:28:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80896>

Here's to forward progress, if even just baby steps! I so need that, for my mental state, if nothing else. Working on sleep and light and reduced EMF, too. For a computer-geek family like mine, that's been a big thing to tackle! But again, baby steps, right???

### diane — 2013-03-17T22:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80898>

Do you have blue blockers Heidi? Those can make a huge difference for light!

### caroline — 2013-03-18T00:06:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80905>

Yes - absolutely - baby steps! For me it has been an accumulation of little things.....lots of times I didn't even notice and then one day you go ...WOW....when did all that happen....we have so much to learn about ourselves and what works for us and I only seem to take on board bits at a time. We have to get thru all the blogs and understand them - and the second/third time around we see something else we didn't quite connect the first time and - as well - by then we are at a different stage in our journey so something stands out more. Then there is everyone's posts with personal experiences that leave a huge imprint on me.

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-18T00:47:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80909>

Yes, I have blue blockers. In fact, the whole family does.... Hubby and all 4 kids. I noticed such a huge difference right away, just within the first few days. I actually get tired now and occasionally my kiddos are the ones putting ME to bed and tucking ME in at night.  It's almost habit now for us to grab them when the sun goes down. Although, The Hubs wouldn't allow me to walk with him through Home Depot with them on the other night. Sheesh! I thought I looked pretty fantastic and made quite the fashion statement... he obviously thought otherwise! LOL.

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-18T00:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-days-for-hormone-panel-testing.6375/post-80910>

Thanks for the encouragement, Caroline. I'm trying so so so hard to sort through all of the blogs. Goodness! My brain just does NOT work the way it used to in my younger years. Or, should I say, in my healthier years. It does get easier with each pass through, that's for sure. I'm picking up on the nuggets of truth here and there, trying to take what applies to me and use it. The blog comments help the most, I think, with providing greater insight and understanding to the posts. Sometimes I just need things re-worded again and again. And again.... and then again!
