# Thread: Input Wanted - Member Projects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-03-18T14:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80961>

I have had this idea for a while, but yesterday I was watching a youtube video and a structure came to mind. I imagine many of you have had a similar idea. Member research projects.

Here's how it could work. A member wants to know more about a specific topic and is willing to work on researching the topic.

Post a project proposal and ask 1) is there interest in the topic and 2) is anyone willing to volunteer to work on the project.

If there are both interest and volunteers, form a project team, define the project, assign tasks, etc, and publish the final report.

I'm purposely leaving out detail so we can build the concept together (if there is interest).

Here's the [video](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=62cNtvx6P8E) that got me motivated. I think if we had a few volunteers, we could develop a map of best and worst places to live, and why.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-18T14:33:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80962>

I like it........and when the research is done.......a collaborative blog can be written by the project leader.........I would be willing to post it. We could call it the Member Series.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T14:55:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80966>

Sounds great! I'd like to participate.

### kathylu — 2013-03-18T15:09:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80967>

I'm in! I love research....

### Dali Dula — 2013-03-18T15:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80971>

I have lately been envisioning a similar concept. I think it would be useful to try to prove Jack wrong on a given subject. This would not be to trash Jack. I am obviously a fan as a result of actionable protocols producing tremendous results for me. This would tie in well with the project concept. By trying to refute a given theory we would come to understand it in greater depth than when we just accept it. Ideally this process would not be emotionally driven. Although, given the amount of data in all subject areas that apparently conflicts with itself this may be a futile exercise.
A group research project of any type sounds like fun. I'm in.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T19:48:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80991>

I have an idea for our initial project. It's one that is timely and requires varying degrees of expertise.

Title: Magnetic, Electric, and Radio/Microwave Measurement Using a Trifield(T) Meter

1) How to use the meter
2) Readings for varying devices/appliances at specific distances

For those who have a meter or want to buy a meter, we would develop a how to tutorial. I have a meter but not really sure how to use it or the data from it. At this point, I'm not getting my money's worth out of it. I can point it at something and read the needle, but what the heck does it mean?

Build a database or spreadsheet of readings. Probably use distances like 0, 1, 2, 3 meters. For people who can't afford or choose not to buy a meter, this would give them ballpark readings they could use to reposition furniture. It would also let people compare their appliances to the average range from data we collect. We would establish the parameters and then get as many people as possible to send us their readings.

This would have a relatively short time requirement and hopefully be available soon after the EMF Webinar.

What is available (free) for collaboration? What tools do we need? Is email and google docs good enough?

I think a project team of 3 to 5 people is right for the 1st part of this project. Send me a PM if you are interested and we can start planning the project.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T19:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-80993>

My above post wasn't meant to stop anyone else from starting a project. My point was that the 1st project will hopefully be able to define tools, etc that other projects can use without having to start from scratch.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-18T20:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81004>

This is a great idea. I would like to participate in some fashion as well. Sounds like we need a project manager to manage the whole thing.

### Hope — 2013-03-18T23:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81021>

Yes, count me in too. Love research and studying. Just have to print and read it all OFFline. LOL.

### Destiny — 2013-03-19T00:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81025>

I am trying to limit my time on computer but I would like to help as well.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-19T01:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81026>

What a fantastic idea. I'd love to help too, but unfortunately I'm in the midst of some huge projects at work right now so I wouldn't be able to help on this particular project. I'd definitely love to help out on a future project though!

### cantweight — 2013-03-19T01:12:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81027>

I'd be in for a summer project. Nursing school kicking my ass till mid May

Christi

### Huck — 2013-03-19T19:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81111>

Need 1 or 2 more people for Magnetic, Electric, and Radio/Microwave Measurement Using a Trifield(T) Meter Project.

EE background would be helpful. If you don't have time for research, but could answer questions and be a technical reviewer, that would be a big help.

### Martin — 2013-03-19T20:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81116>

Great idea, Huck. I'm looking at making body voltage measuring device using inexpensive parts and a decent multi-meter. I'm wanting to post pics so others can build it as well and save money and learn something.

I'm looking at gauss meters and how to get the most for my money. I love good tools and have made my living with the best I could afford, which means compromising somewhat if it will only be used a few times. On tools that I use constantly, getting a great one was an investment.

I like the Tri-Field, but according to inspectors who go out and measure for clients, it has limitations. But, like I said, we ain't going into the biz of getting hired and going out into the field. Then again... it could be an emerging occupation!

I'm looking at ones in the $250 range. Pricey compared to many others, it's inexpensive compared to professional models that cost thousands! I want accuracy, reliability, repeatability, and some tech support.

I have an idea of offering basic preliminary on-site measuring for the people in my town for two reasons - spread the awareness and help pay for the meter. I don't know how much people want this investigated, but I'd think it would be fun to go and show people this.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-20T15:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81178>

Everyone who is interested, please PM Huck if you haven't done so already.

### CarolMorris — 2013-03-20T17:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/input-wanted-member-projects.6380/post-81185>

Wonderful idea...I would love to contribute...there are so many great people on this forum that really "get it" and I struggle constantly with the science. I found my way here  that's a good thing and I am extremely grateful for all of you and Dr. Jack!! I'm willing to give it my best...give me the grunt work Anyone else feel this way?

### nonchalant — 2013-03-19T17:21:25+0200

### Jonathin — 2013-03-19T18:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-pollack-structured-water-grounding-interview-by-dr-mercola.6384/post-81106>

[http://faculty.washington.edu/ghp/](http://faculty.washington.edu/ghp/)

Pollack has been researching water scientifically for over a decade at the U of Washington. He also puts on the water conference in New England every year. His book Cells, Gels and the Engines of Life is a good layman oriented view of water and how cells work. I got a hold of a complete copy of his yet to be released book The Fourth Phase of Water. In it he details the multi-layered "chicken wire" mesh that structured water forms along hydrophillic surfaces. It is very fascinating and he goes into many areas like ice formation, clouds, protonated water (hydronium ion), pH changes and much more. Personally I consider the information in this book to be pivotal to understanding the natural world. Nothing airy fairy here just solid research about the unasked questions about the fundamental nature of water. Hopefully it will be available very soon.

### cinnamon — 2013-03-19T21:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-pollack-structured-water-grounding-interview-by-dr-mercola.6384/post-81120>

I found this 2 part video on youtube of Dr. Pollack speaking at the Electric Universe Conference in January 2013. Some of the material is the same as in the Mercola interview, but there are slides that help for those who learn visually.

Part 1: [http://youtu.be/JnGCMQ8TJ\_g](http://youtu.be/JnGCMQ8TJ_g)

Part 2: [http://youtu.be/hqHWueBp23c](http://youtu.be/hqHWueBp23c)

There is a lot of good stuff here. After watching, I wondered if maybe one of the good things about CT is that it might help restructure the water in our cells.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-20T17:42:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-pollack-structured-water-grounding-interview-by-dr-mercola.6384/post-81189>

It is not a technical book really. High school science might be a prerequisite. The mind blowing part is that the properties and interactions of water has never been thoroughly investigated until recently. Even the phenomenon of osmosis seems to have been based on a number of assumptions that are not really true. Personally, I think it is a must read for anybody that thinks the physical world is an interesting place.

# Thread: Mg & Vitamin D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### enyaw — 2013-03-18T18:32:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-80983>

If I'm taking Magnesium three times a day, when is a good time to take a vitamin D supplement?

### freshveggies — 2013-03-18T20:36:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81000>

> [MooseMan said:](/goto/post?id=80983)
>
> If I'm taking Magnesium three times a day, when is a good time to take a vitamin D supplement?

1st thing in the morning. Then wait 2 hours before you take magnesium.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-18T20:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81001>

I take my morning Mg then wait about 3 hours before taking my Vit D then another 3-4 hours before the next dose of Mg.

### NittDan — 2013-03-19T02:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81039>

Is not D absorbed best with a meal? Take with your BAB, then wait a few hours to take Mg, as Patty Cake says.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-19T02:32:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81040>

What's the reason for waiting between taking D and Mg?

### janagram — 2013-03-21T07:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81277>

Awe, heck. Been doing that wrong....

### enyaw — 2013-03-21T13:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81285>

Vitamin D and magnesium fight for absorption, and magnesium wins.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T14:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81286>

I was not aware of that. Does anyone have a cite?

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-21T16:11:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81296>

Dr. Kruse has said that to me in a consult...I've separated them for the last six months.

### Vicster — 2013-03-21T16:40:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81298>

My powder LEF Neuro-Mag has 1000 iu of D3. If Mg causes a problem with absorption of D, why would they add it?

### Jonathin — 2013-03-21T17:54:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81319>

You have the powder not the capsules. Capsules only have mag threonate. Powder also has calcium.

### Terminator — 2013-03-21T18:20:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81325>

Can you guys tell me how Magnesium Malate tastes ? I got this brand "Magnesium Malate Powder Nature's Life 360g Powder", it tastes horrible. Maybe like ammonia. I just want to know if i was scammed.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-21T18:40:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81329>

I'm not sure why LEF is combining them....but then again multi vitimins often have combinations that cancel each other out...LEF also puts potato maltodextrin, rice powder, soybean and other not so great stuff in some formulas and other ones are great....I guess we just have to be vigilant and picky about where we get our supplements. I order from at least five different companies for the things I take.

### Sistagrok — 2013-03-21T19:17:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81335>

> [marcoc said:](/goto/post?id=81325)

I use magnesium malate powder from Beyond a Century and it has almost no taste at all, so maybe that ammonia taste smell may not be so good?

### janagram — 2013-03-23T06:52:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81514>

my tablet doesn't disintegrate so I don't get much taste but now that u mention it, I may have detected something like that a time or two..so the powder...could taste like that.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-23T17:19:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-81539>

[http://pforlife.com/magnesium-malate-dimagnesium-malate-20powder.html](http://pforlife.com/magnesium-malate-dimagnesium-malate-20powder.html)

Practically, no taste.

### melrito — 2013-05-15T18:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-86098>

**taste**

> [marcoc said:](/goto/post?id=81325)
>
> Can you guys tell me how Magnesium Malate tastes ? I got this brand "Magnesium Malate Powder Nature's Life 360g Powder", it tastes horrible. Maybe like ammonia. I just want to know if i was scammed.

I use the same one as you and it doesn't taste like anything to me...

### Terminator — 2013-05-17T23:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-86344>

Sistagrok,
I bought magnesium malate powder from Beyond a Century and i believe it's just white sand not real magnesium. Why ? I put a half of teaspoon into a cup of water and it did not desolve even after more than 24 hours. They refunded and i did not have to return it. Supplement industry is very unregulated so you end up bying crap from Chine many times.

### Jonathin — 2013-05-18T19:22:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-86421>

Marcoc - Why do you think it should dissolve in water? My recommendation above is from Albion Labs and is USP. It does not really dissolve. The conditions of the gut are quite different from a glass of room temperature water.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-18T20:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mg-vitamin-d.6385/post-86426>

> [Vicster said:](/goto/post?id=81298)
>
> My powder LEF Neuro-Mag has 1000 iu of D3. If Mg causes a problem with absorption of D, why would they add it?

Possibly because magnesium is a cofactor for utilizing vitamin D. Arguably, some suggested side effects of vitamin D might actually be signs of magnesium deficiency. D also enhances calcium transporters which I believe competes with magnesium absorption so perhaps the maker includes mag as insurance to prevent excessive calcification by those not getting enough menatetranone (K2 MK4).

### Hope — 2013-03-19T01:57:26+0200

# Thread: Saltwater Pool
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-18T19:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-80992>

Does anyone have experience with a saltwater pool? We currently have a "shared" aboveground pool with my sister's family next door. I'm considering trying to get them to allow me to pay to convert it to saltwater. They do most of the maintenance on the pool since it's in their backyard, but we do split all costs with them.

I've been doing some research and talked to a few reps at pool supply stores and I get the feeling they're trying to talk me out of it. Most admit there may be a bit less maintenance, but all say we're still generating chlorine so the chemical effect is no different.

I stumbled across this other option as well: [http://www.ecosmartepool.com/pool-spa-faq.html](http://www.ecosmartepool.com/pool-spa-faq.html) Has anyone ever heard of the EcoSmarte pools?

I did swim quite a bit last year in the chlorinated pool and always felt better when swimming, but my gut told me that it wasn't good and of course Jack has confirmed that quite a few times. I hate the thought of my kids swimming in that every day this summer too.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-18T20:50:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-81005>

Just a thought....we use silver to treat our hot/cold tub

[http://www.ehow.com/way\_5538843\_can-colloidal-silver-treat-pool.html](http://www.ehow.com/way_5538843_can-colloidal-silver-treat-pool.html)

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-18T20:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-81006>

I've been looking into the ecosmarte system as well. I refuse to go back to using chlorine in my pool. If I have no other choice, I won't open it this year.

### NittDan — 2013-03-19T02:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-81036>

Ditto to what Cindy says. No dry skin either. Have been swimming in mine for 7 years.

### Jude — 2013-03-19T03:22:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-81050>

> [CindyB said:](/goto/post?id=81022)
>
> I have a salt water pool. It is swimming in a saline solution. Rock salt is dissolved in the water. The sanitation aspect happens in the mechanical area where the pumps, etc. are located. The system does generate chlorine gas which then decontaminates the water in the near vicinity of the generation point but the Cl2 decomposes quickly (within about 8-10" of the generation system). The system pumps the salty pool water through the generation system which converts the salt's chlorine ion into Cl2. No chlorine gas ever leaves the generation system. You do not swim with any Cl2. You swim in salty water.
>
> That being said, the decontamination activity only happens when the pump is running the water through the generator. When the pump is off, there is no additional decontamination. A traditional chlorine-based swimming pool has enough chlorine gas dissolved in the water that it continues to de-contaminate even if there is no water circulation happening.
>
> There are still times when a salt water pool will need chlorine poured in to deal with a rampant problem (e.g. little kiddies with bowel training issues, etc.)
>
> Love the salt water pool. Easy to float. No burning eyes. Blond hair does not turn green. No obnoxious chlorine smell on your skin.

Thanks for that info. I guess all salt pools operate on that principle? Never thought to check all those details out-just presumed it was salty with no chlorine--never smelt any in 32 yrs....

### Ariel8100 — 2013-04-12T21:41:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saltwater-pool.6387/post-83934>

Owning a swimming pool is an investment in both time and money, but is also a fun activity for every member of your family. Converting a pool requires you to replace the original chlorinator with a salt-based chlorinator that converts table salt into natural chlorine.
I agreed what is said above!!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-looking-for-a-coach.6390/post-81015>

Hello,
I'm new here, but not entirely new to these ideas. I am a busy stay at home mom, trying to start my own business- and I just do not have the time to figure out Jack's website by myself! I'm looking to hire a coach who can help me through this process, and can coach me through getting my hormones and health back on track.

Here is a little about me. I am almost 48 years old, not exhibiting perimenopausal symtoms yet, but I know I must be close. I weigh 240 lbs (as of this AM) and my weight continues to creep up. This is my biggest issue. My entire adult life until about age 39- my weight ranged from 145-170.Then I got pregnant-- gained 70 lbs, got pregnant again.. and have 2 lovely children. I thought I settled at 205-220. Tried HCG to lose the rest... got down to 190. and now am back up. I do have some emotional addictive eating problems too I guess.

Huge weight swings as you can see. I have always loved jumping in cold rivers lake and oceans. So I'm attracted to the ideas of CT.

I'm certain that I'm leptin resistant, and am sure that my hormones are out of whack., I eat fairly healthy- paleo/ weston price guided- but then will binge on girlscout cookies, or my kid's snacks. I'd love some help with a coach or practitioner who works with women my ageish, with similar issues.

Any recommendations? Does Jack even work with people directly? I just can't get through everything on his blog. it's too much for me and i have too much other stuff going on. Thanks so much.

Shauna
[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) or [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

You need to start the leptin reset and pay attention to light cycles and read about C/T and pay attention to EMF's .... and then just jump in.
There is a ton of reading for sure - but the easy start guides will get you going ASAP. Go to the home page for the guides......

Welcome Shauna and good luck!

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-19T15:45:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-plan-to-chart-heart-risk-in-1-million-adults-in-real-time.6395/post-81081>

[http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424127887324323904578368572640617966.html](http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424127887324323904578368572640617966.html)

Researchers are launching a major study that will marshal the power of smartphones and other personal technologies in an effort to develop new strategies for preventing and managing heart disease.

The project, called the Health eHeart Study, will use tools such as smartphone apps, sensors and other devices to gather data on a wide variety of measures associated with cardiovascular healthâ€”including blood pressure, physical activity, diet and sleep habitsâ€”all in real time.

**The initiative amounts to "a large-scale digital version of the Framingham Heart Study,"** said Jeffrey Olgin, chief of cardiology at University of California, San Francisco and co-principal investigator of the effort. The continuing 65-year-old Framingham study is one of the most influential research endeavors in medical history.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Yikes, can you imagine the EMFs the participants will encounter?? Hmm, I wonder if the study includes that important impact?

> The study, funded so far by private philanthropy,

sounds great, but personally I smell a dirty rat that has been sucking unstructured water from cell phone industry's commodes.

my company is kicking of a health initiative...

wellness initiative kicks-off with a seminar on May 30th by Dr. Michael Greger titled â€œThe Role of Diet in Preventing, Arresting, and Reversing the 15 Leading Causes of Death,â€￾ followed by a 9-week course taught by a Physicians Committee for Responsible Medicine (PCRM) Food for Life cooking instructor, Kara Blank-Gonzalez. Yesterday, there was a very young & fit woman set up with a table near the employee entrance. She was there to sign people up for this (which is also posted on the employee website):

Are you interested in healthy eating, but not sure where to start? Want to learn the differences between:
• Nutrient-dense vs. calorie-dense foods
• Complex vs. refined carbohydrates
• Lean proteins vs. high fat proteins
• Healthy fats vs. saturated or trans fats

There’s no time like the present to look at your nutritional health. Please join us for a wellness seminar, “Simplify, Substitute and Satisfy” presented by xxxxxxxxxxxxx, our Cigna On-Site Health Promotion Coordinator.

When: March 21 or April 4 from 12:00 to 1:00 PM
Where: The Training Room – The Woodlands
Seating is limited. Please RSVP to [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]). Bring your lunch and see if it’s as healthy as you think.

I stopped by her table and after looking at the flyers said, "I don't eat this way. I eat epi-paleo and with lots of fat."

### Hope — 2013-03-19T23:34:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seriously-this-is-what-is-being-promoted-as-healthy.6402/post-81133>

Yep. They really believe in this stuff. I have heard him (NB) speak and he has some cases that actually change their lives around. I don't think it's forever though. But, for some people it's better than the SAD diet. Howard Lyman, a rancher (featured on Oprah in the 90s) says he wouldn't be alive today if he didn't turn vegan. # Thread: Triglycerides really up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2013-03-19T22:40:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81128>

I just got my cholesterol back and my triglycerids are horendoues. I have been on a ketogenic diet - mostly epi-paleo since October 2012. I am also taking Cytomel. I am not sure why my triglycerides went up so much. I am not blaming my diet, I just want to understand this. I went from 142-122 lbs in 4 months. Coudl this be the reason? How should I bring my triglycerides down? Thank you!

The tests from March 2013 and April 2012 were done by the same lab. The ranges are at the end.

**Cholesterol** - March 2013 â€“ **210 High**, April 2012 â€“ 219 High (< 200 mg/dl)
**HDL** â€“ March 2013 â€“ **61**; April 2012 â€“ 71 (>50)
**Triglycerides** â€“ March 2013 â€“ **206 High**; April 2012 â€“ 55 (<150 mg/dl)
**LDL** â€“ March 2013 â€“ **108 High**; April 2012 â€“ 137 High (<100 mg/dl)
**VLDL, Calculated** â€“ March 2013 â€“ **41 High**; April 2012 â€“ 11 (7-32 mg/dl)

**Chol/HDL ratio** â€“ March 2013 â€“ **3.4**; April 2012 - 3.1 (<5.8)
**LDL/HDL ratio** â€“ March 2013 -**1.77**; April 2012 â€“ 1.93 (.56)

### shilohman — 2013-03-20T01:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81136>

High TG is an indication that your liver is working poorly. High TG and LDL indicates that 1) your lipoprotein lipase LPL is not working well at storing fatty acids into your fat cells 2) your magnesium levels are low 3) CoenzQ10 is low 4) your L-carnitine levels and carnitine shuttle is trashed and 5) D Ribose is low.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-20T01:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81139>

Not necessarily ... my last check my Mg was high and so were my trigs. I've been eating a boatload of butter and bacon fat AND taking milk thistle to boot. I've upped my coq10 to 300 mg /day and supplement with acetyl l carnitine AND d ribose. I posted my current numbers over in my [journal](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?627-So-let's-try-this-DarleenMB&p=81124#post81124).

### Destiny — 2013-03-20T02:04:19+0200

I posted my current numbers over in my [journal](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?627-So-let's-try-this-DarleenMB&p=81124#post81124).

Darleen, I checked your cholesterol results and they are somehow similar to mine including higher triglycerides.

### Destiny — 2013-03-20T02:06:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81143>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=81136)
>
> High TG is an indication that your liver is working poorly. High TG and LDL indicates that 1) your lipoprotein lipase LPL is not working well at storing fatty acids into your fat cells 2) your magnesium levels are low 3) CoenzQ10 is low 4) your L-carnitine levels and carnitine shuttle is trashed and 5) D Ribose is low.

The question is why my liver function went down. It was a bit better 11 months ago.

### kathylu — 2013-03-20T03:22:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81153>

Destiny, are you actively losing weight? That can cause high trigs. Should be temporary though.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-20T03:42:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81155>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=81143)
>
> The question is why my liver function went down. It was a bit better 11 months ago.

Are you taking any medications? Many meds can stress your liver. As Shilohman said, high TG is a sign of poor liver function. I would suggest researching any meds you are or have taken for potential side effects on your liver.

In my N=1, the combo of T3 and Prometrium caused liver stress for me.

Perhaps a liver detox might help. [http://www.vitaminstohealth.com/liver-detoxification.html](http://www.vitaminstohealth.com/liver-detoxification.html)

### shilohman — 2013-03-20T04:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81158>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=81143)
>
> The question is why my liver function went down. It was a bit better 11 months ago.

Can't say why your liver function went down, but it is obvious you are not in the Pentose Phosphate Pathway (PPP), so I would recommend that you recheck your diet and make sure you are eating VERY high fat, medium protein and low carbs. Also drink as much spring/RO water as you can.

I stopped losing weight two months ago. I am now very stable at 122. Maybe I need more than two months.

>

There might be something to that. I just restarted progesterone. I also take Metformin 1000 mg a day since last September.

### Destiny — 2013-03-20T12:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81164>

You are right, that is quite obvious. I need to work on more fat even though I am not really hungry. I am good with protein and very low carb - did not have any fruit not even a lick since last October. I am really very disciplined that way.

I just started with spring water 10 days ago.

### shilohman — 2013-03-20T17:01:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81181>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=81164)
>
> You are right, that is quite obvious. I need to work on more fat even though I am not really hungry. I am good with protein and very low carb - did not have any fruit not even a lick since last October. I am really very disciplined that way.
>

From my personal experience, the big difference in my energy level was when I upped my water intake to 90-100 ounces of water a day.
It was almost like flicking a switch. To figure out what your minimum water intake should be just divide your body weight by 2 and that will
give you your target. I weigh 180 lbs and had been consuming 60-80 oz. of water. Upping it to 90-100 has made a big difference for me.

### CarolMorris — 2013-03-20T17:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81188>

Destiny you must be so frustrated...doing everything you know that is right and not getting the results you expect. I wish I had an answer for you. Hang in there!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/triglycerides-really-up.6403/post-81235>

> [CarolM said:](/goto/post?id=81188)

You would think so, Carol, but I am not. I just would like to solve this puzzle. Our systems are so simple when they work and so complex when they don't.

# Thread: Has limiting my EMF and flouride exposure temporarily trashed my leptin sensitivity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FreeRangeKiwi — 2013-03-20T08:35:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81161>

Hey guys, it's time for me to eat humble pie. Until recently I've had good results. I've been eating sub 10g carb, high seafood, high fat, diurnally ever since Sept 2011. I CT'd all last year. It is coming to the end of summer here in the South of NZ and I'm about to hit the ocean a whole lot more frequently. The last few months it had been an every third day affair.

I have felt like a rockstar up until now. But in the last month I've lost so many of the gains I had made. My guess is (no labs drawn) that I've got trashed vitD, DHEA and cortisol rhythm. I'm a 33 year old male, have gained 4-6kg (circa 10lb), lost my normal emotional stability, got cold hands/feet, IBS returned, and am wide awake (although refreshed) after 4 hours of sleep. it takes me 3hours to get back to sleep (fyi, don't try Tim Ferriss' high fat spoonful technique in the middle of the night - it works, but you feel rotten when you wake up).

Things I'm doing:
\*I'm turning off the mains board at night
\*sunnies on in evening
\*grounding lots during the day - i look like a bloody hippie walking in barefeet everywhere
\*bed soon after the light falls
\*I'm drinking spring water only
\*reduced coffee to one in the morning
\*eating heaps of fat
\*having a salty bone broth (NZ beef or lamb) in the morning
\*having seaweed, possibly not enough
\*not struggling to go without food all day, but ghrelin seems to get active after I wake at 2am.
\*bigger BAB than you (possibly too big - up to 100g protein)
\*sunbath even on cold days

i'm not:
\*supping with anything - stopped in Nov
\*getting the sunlight i was getting 1 month ago
\*exercising more than every second day, often only one weight session a week
\*grounding at night
\*totally free of technology yet during the day
\*CTing more than twice a week
\*eating seasonal carbs

the significant changes i've made in this period (last month + 1/2) are to reduce EMF exposure and remove fluoride. My feeling is that now that these two factors have been significantly reduced, body needs more iodine to fill the void - more seaweed you say?. thoughts? also, have i confused my brain by not having the seasonal carbs as per the post leptin reset rx? i see there is a question put to JK about the possibility of initial adverse effects - what do all you brainiacs reckon?

### Destiny — 2013-03-20T12:30:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81166>

It looks that you know what is your problem. Are you thinking with starting supplementing D3, DHEA and add Lugol and see how that goes?

I remember when we here were starting the winter cycle in the northern hemisphere, many of us started to feel not that great!

### Huck — 2013-03-20T14:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81170>

I started having problems the very beginning of fall and it has lasted all winter. I'm hoping longer light cycles will get things going again.

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T14:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81171>

I stopped supplementing my whole family last summer thinking we didn't need it as we were outside constantly....our D levels plummeted. I was shocked. Went from 50-60 range to teens and 20's. Obviously still not able to assimilate the D they way I expected we would.

### Huck — 2013-03-20T14:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81173>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=81171)
>
> I stopped supplementing my whole family last summer thinking we didn't need it as we were outside constantly....our D levels plummeted. I was shocked. Went from 50-60 range to teens and 20's. Obviously still not able to assimilate the D they way I expected we would.

Until I got my D into the 60's with supplements, the sun didn't seem to have much, if any, effect. I'm sure it is more complicated than that, but for several years I tried to raise my vitamin D by spending hours in the sun and it barely budged. Once I got it into 60's to low 70's using supplements, the sun moved it up to 110 over the summer.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-20T15:28:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81175>

Well, I'm the grounding nut here, so I think it's grounding.
My experience at night on the sheet is incredibly different from walking barefoot, unless I'm walking on wet ground. Perhaps bobbing the harbor would be on par, but I get 8-9 hours solid grounding at night. Plus hours more during the day while sitting at the computer sitting on my grounding sheet with feet on a pad, plus some barefoot time.

Perhaps it's a mitochondria problem. You need support for them so that they retain the charge you give them with grounding, sunning, structured water, seaweed, fats, etc. If you don't take any supps, you will probably need to get very cold, very dark at night, very grounded. And maybe magnets, though I don't have experience with them.

I also had difficulty in the autumn and winter. I think if we are optimal during the summer, we will sail through winter. Optimal Vitamin D, optimal hormones, optimal mitochondria, optimal leptin.

### HoneyChild — 2013-03-20T17:56:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-limiting-my-emf-and-flouride-exposure-temporarily-trashed-my-leptin-sensitivity.6407/post-81192>

Perhaps you're like Jack and need a LOT more CT...

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthre...thought-I-would-share-for-educational-purpose](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3884-I-received-my-new-set-of-labs-today-and-thought-I-would-share-for-educational-purpose)

# Thread: Hello, from Oregon!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-03-20T12:20:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81165>

I'm a 48 yo female with Lyme disease and Fibromyalgia (with PTSD). I started a dairy free paleo diet again, am taking Mag Malate, Mag Threonate, high dose fish oil (1325 mg tid), Armour Thyroid 60mg, Kelp. I haven't started CT yet. Which protocol should I start first? Is there a way to combine them?

### fitness@home — 2013-03-20T13:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81169>

Welcome! There are many here combining protocols. I would do the Leptin Reset protocol with BAB (big @ss breakfast) and Epi-Paleo. Eating more seafood is better than supplementing with a lot of fish oil. Think fish oil in its natural package!

Begin minding your light cycles and wear blue-blockers after dark. You can also start becoming aware of the amount of EMFs in your environment. Turn off wifi at home, unplug appliances that are not in use, remove TVs and other electronics from your bedroom. Some people here turn off the breakers in their homes at night.

Ease into CT when you start and read, read, read. If you have questions post them on the forum and you will receive feedback.

Good luck on your journey!

p.s. Forgot to add drink lots of springwater, especially if you have flouridated "city" water.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-20T15:38:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81176>

Welcome memullin! Glad to have you here.
Fitness@home gave some great advice. Fixing your circadian rhythm will allow your body to coordinate the release of various hormones at the right time and allow you to repair your body at night, when all the conditions are right. Excellent sleep is the most important thing, so that you can repair and also regenerate your energy from ATP.

One other small thing to add at the beginning, along with seafood and good water, is sunlight and walking barefoot. So you can work on the Leptin reset protocol, but add those other factors, so that you get a head start on the other protocols.

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-20T19:34:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81201>

Thanks you two! I forgot to add that I'm 5'6 1/2" and 255 lbs. 15 of which I gained on my overnight job as a gluten free chef this past year. Also, about 9 months ago I had a total hysterectomy and am without a gallbladder. It gets worse! 2 or 3 days back into Paleo I got my first "smart" phone. After feeling remarkably better on the diet I suddenly felt awful again! Problem is, that's how I read Jack's info. So now I've been voraciously reading up on the articles in the middle of the night again, but this time it's closer to my head. I was supposed to be on day by now, but it looks like it's going to another month.

I was out of work for 4 years because I was so sick with the Lyme and severe microcytic anemia (which supposedly is now gone.) When I went back to work my one stipulation was that it had to be day shift. I was hired for day shift but never got to work it. I was immediately put on evenings, had my hysterectomy, and then put on graveyards. This last year has been hell! I don't know I got through it, but God got me through. Now I'm being teased by the promise
of going to day shift and am at the end of my rope. My bosses aren't lyi ng to me. We're still waiting for permits to expand the kitchen. Btw, I've been writing all this with numb fingers and a numb hand from carpal tunnel syndrome.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-20T19:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81205>

If the shift change doesn't happen soon, you may want to consider looking for another position. Your health is important! Shift work is hard on your body.

Do you have someone with a computer/printer setup? You could print out the blogs and read the hard copy version. That is what I do...just easier on my eyes. Of course, I am fortunate because I work in an office and print them out at work.

Hang in there.

### caroline — 2013-03-20T23:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-oregon.6408/post-81225>

Welcome - everyone has given you great advice. Hang in there ......

# Thread: Really on the fence about trying hcg again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 21

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T19:46:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81204>

I have a few vials of hcg that I have been holding on to for over a year....they are calling my name louder and louder as summer creeps closer and I can't stand carrying this weight around. I have done multiple rounds of hcg no problem phase 1 or 2. Weight loss every time 35 lbs on a 40 day run. Phase 3 trips me up every time. I dont do steak days or weigh every day. I stick to the proper foods and within about 3 weeks gain back every lb and then some. This has happened all 3 times. And I behave like a beast when dosing. My family cringed when I said I wanted to try again. The only way they agreed to go along with it is if I sign a contract that states i will discontinue use immediately if my behavior is unbearable again.

Has anyone experienced similar failures with hcg but succeeded when incorporating CT and leptin rx or epi paleo phase 3?

Thanks so much for your time and input. Really struggling with this decision. Waiting on my email consult and 60 min w/ Dr. Kruse....will see what he thinks as well.

Christi

### fitness@home — 2013-03-20T19:54:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81206>

Christi,

My first round I lost 15 lbs and the LIW became my new set point. That was two years ago. I flucutate 1-2 lbs up/down from it. I have eaten paleo/epi-paleo with lots of fat since then.

I did a 2nd round about six months after the first one and only dropped about 7 lbs (but a lot of inches). Over the next 16 months it _slowly_ came back. I truly think my body wasn't ready for another new set point yet.

Like you, I also had boxes of HCG left. Decided to do a round and am 12 days into it. My own little N=1. Drinking lots of spring water and grounding daily. So far, so good. I feel like my body is ready to accept a new set point now.

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T19:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81207>

Thats kind of what I was thinking....if i am hydrated, CTing, making good (seafood) protein choices, supplementing, grounding....maybe Id see different results. For weeks now my body has had no desire to eat. I have gagged while trying to prepare a BAB, feel so sick after eating it....just zero appetite. Am starting to think maybe i should work with what my bod is giving me?

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T20:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81209>

The whole hcg increased sex drive thing has me a little freaked too. My sex drive is already absurd. If it got stronger I am afraid I may spontaneously combust!!

### caroline — 2013-03-21T00:02:33+0200

Some people have all the luck!

Just curious.....why don't you do steak days or weight yourself every day? I would be lost without those two tools after HCG. I always need/ want to keep on top of any weight gain right away and fix it ASAP. My weight is now pretty stable.

### IceBarbie — 2013-03-21T06:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81274>

I found each subsequent round of hcg even harder to finish. But this last round went very well. The difference? I started an Amino Acid protocol outlined in The Diet Cure by Julia Ross. She explains how dieting depletes our levels of seratonin etc. She has a clinic in CA with a very high success rate for eating disorders, alcoholism, drug abuse etc. All of these issues have the same underlying cause--a malnourished brain. I read the book, took notes and ordered the supplements and aminos that addressed my personal issues. For me, this included food cravings of sugar, carbs & chocolate that led to bingeing all my life (but I didn't realize it was an eating disorder--I just thought I had poor eating habits) plus other issues such as anxiety. The idea is to bring your seratonin or dopamine levels up by adding amino acids while you change your diet to include at least 3 servings of 20-30 grams of protein each day spaced 4-5 hours apart to continually nourish your brain. In addition, eat lots of colorful vegetables and some fruit. Avoid grains if you are sensitive to them. This treatment usually lasts 3-12 months for most people who can then let diet alone take over while phasing out the supplements once they feel normal again. I highly recommend her book if you have been dieting a long time. Even tho I was on hcg, I did use the aminos. I also upped my protein a tad and left out one fruit and the grissino or melba as I am sensitive to wheat or gluten. The amino supplementation made a huge difference. I feel like I am finally getting my eating under control and it's only been 3-4 weeks since I started using them per Ross's protocol. Someone recommended this book to me months ago and I could kick myself for waiting so long to read it. By the way, I started at 220 lbs and am at 143 lbs as of today. I was at a lower weight last year but started gaining because I couldn't stay away from sugar etc. So I have about 10 lbs left to lose to get back into my summer clothes. I hope this helps you or anyone else with similar issues as mine.

### cantweight — 2013-03-21T21:55:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81353>

Caroline..I never really decided not to do weigh or do steak days; I just always squeezed my hcg rounds in between things like vactions, holidays, birthdays, busy times and once I was no longer injecting and got preoccupied with life I got lax about it and the weight came back so fast and furiously that by the time I checked the scale 1-2 lbs had long passed. Something i will rectify this time around and see if it helps.

IceBarbie that is really interesting. My Daughter has anxiety issues and we have put her on a protocol of B12, L-Theanine, and Taurine. I wonder if these are the amino acids you used as well? I love the L-Theanine 2 of those and i feel like Ive had a cup of coffee and glass of wine....love it.

I was thinking about approaching the hcg a little differently than i have in the past. Have all my protein within a 1/2 hour of waking. Have my coffee with tsp HWC. No breadstick. Fruit for dinner. CT at least 3 times a week. I will keep the amino acids in mind if the hormones and not eating affect my moods. Think I am going to do it. Really cant not bear to carry this weight anymore. I am similar in size to you. At 260 right now...ugh!

### curves64 — 2013-03-23T21:37:16+0200

I have been puzzling over how to mesh hcg and Dr. K's prescriptions as well. I had a lot of success losing in the past but, like all of you, had trouble maintaining that loss for long periods of time. IN fact, I just aborted a round as it has the opposite effect on me as cantweight. It decreases my ability to have an orgasm. ### IceBarbie — 2013-03-24T03:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81591>

Are there any women on this site who have successfully lost weight without HCG using only Jack's ideas? CT made my thighs even fatter so I was wondering who may have a real success story of beating obesity here that I can read about. One day Jack supports HCG, then the next day he's dissing it again as "unsustainable." The only reason it hasn't been perfect for me (and I've kept about 80 out of 90 lbs off), is because of my sugar/carb craving issues that I have had as long as I can remember. The Amino Acid protocol I am now on is helping beautifully. Julia Ross's The Diet Cure is more about cravings and other eating disorders (and drug & alcohol issues etc) while her book, The Mood Cure is more about emotional issues including depression, anti-depressants plus chemical dependency. I've read them both and found them to be excellent.

I did do a round of hcg while trying to apply Jack's protocol and I did it by eating protein as soon as I got up. Normally, you don't really eat breakfast on hcg. And then I didn't allow any food after 7 pm. I think I allowed a little extra protein too and skipped one fruit. Even with trying to do it his way, I still continued to struggle. My last round of hcg while taking various aminos that fit my issues was my easiest round so far. I've been dealing with a painful sciatic issue since late December and started gaining while being laid up. It's going on 3 months now. Thankfully, I've been able to take that 20 lbs back off with hcg and am back to where I was before this issue started.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T06:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81599>

Colleen Coble is the expert in all things HCG. As far as I am concerned she is a fountain of information. I don't know if she still comes on this forum or not. Maybe Check the HCG forum.

I did hhcg and then went directly into the reset - but you have to be very vigilant. I monitored my weight every day and then if I needed to - I would do a steak day. I never let it get away from me. In my opinion - I think we are pretty unstable after HCG so we really have to be on top of it for at least six months.

I found the BAB difficult but all cravings went away within a week or two which was huge for me and I went to two meals a day pretty quickly. I eat dinner around 3-4:30pm and am not hungry until the next morning. I used to be really bad at raiding the fridge/pantry in the middle of the night so that was huge too.

The only cravings really that I had left was apples/fruit and once I gave up fruit I am now rock solid. I do have some once in a long while and I am fine.

Now I seem to be eating a lot and my weight is drifting downward. I think the spring water is part of it and all the seafood and raw meat. My weight is now 56 K - probably haven't been that weight since I was 12.

In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein. Also follow the leptin Rex exactly. Shift down to two meals when you can and absolutely no snacking.

The other important thing for me was to add more fat and take away some protein which made me feel much better and not so overstuffed.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-24T14:24:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81603>

"In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein."

Caroline, I delete the bread & one fruit and also add some extra protein. This works best for me too. My extra protein is usually some of the ground beef jerky I make. Nibble on it during my hour long commute from work.

I am by know means perfect with the protocol, but it works for me. Love the inch loss and am already wearing some things that haven't been worn in quite some time. Keeping myself well-hydrated with spring water and grounding daily.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T23:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81650>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=81603)
>
> "In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein."
>
> Caroline, I delete the bread & one fruit and also add some extra protein. This works best for me too. My extra protein is usually some of the ground beef jerky I make. Nibble on it during my hour long commute from work.
>
> I am by know means perfect with the protocol, but it works for me. Love the inch loss and am already wearing some things that haven't been worn in quite some time. Keeping myself well-hydrated with spring water and grounding daily.

In my view - I see so many people tinker with dr. Simeon's protocols and for the life of me I don't understand why. It works beautifully.......but so many people try to add in other things like tuna etc. I, too, had 1 apple a day but if I ever need to do it again I would delete all fruit since I think it is a problem for me re - sugar cravings.

Fitness - how will you stabilize after you are done? I think that is wha Dr. K is saying......HCG does work but you really have to work at it afterwards to maintain until you stabilize. It actually isn't that hard but you have to be present and really think about what you are doing. It isn't magic - if you want the weight gone - it is hard work but doable.

I see the same thing with the leptin r/x ..... If you keep falling off the wagon you keep resetting yourself back to the beginning and not getting anywhere. For me - this all had to be priority#1 if I wanted to achieve results. What is it that JK says? Something like ....food shouldn't be your hobby. There is more but I can't remember the exact quote - it really struck home with me.

How do you make ground beef jerky? I would love that! I guess you have a dehydrator.

Isn't it fantastic when clothes start fitting nicely? I love it when they Just hang off you. It gives you so much more confidence - it makes all the difference .....I guess that is a woman thing?

### fitness@home — 2013-03-25T00:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81652>

Caroline, I will send you a message tomorrow with the base recipe. It is on my computer at work. I have some ground chicken "jerky" going in the oven right now. Lowest heat setting for about 7-8 hours.

I stabilize in P3 by eating higher protein/fat ( sound familiar?) You just have to be very strict for the first three weeks and I kind of limit my choices...don't introduce a bunch of thing all at once. I stay away from pretty much all carbs, including any fruit during this period.

After that, it is just my normal way of eating. Seafood, meat, fats - ghee, coconut oil, and rendered fats. Add in some veggies and fruit at times. I am not perfect in this...there are times I will eat something not on the above list like dairy or occasionally eggs on the weekend in the form of breakfast tacos. The problems start when you indulge too often.

This round is helping me clean house so to speak. It is getting my eating habits back on track. Two of my friends have recently started rounds because they've seen how it has worked for me (and they know I research _everything_ thoroughly first).

I can tell you that I am missing my cocoa crack a lot right now . Can't wait to add that back to my diet after completing this round...

### caroline — 2013-03-25T00:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81658>

OMG .... I have forgotten about cocoa crack! I love it - but have a hard time staying away from it.

I am certainly not perfect either - by a long shot - but I weigh everyday to keep on top of it. Lately I seem to have more leeway. I eat a fair bit of Stilton cheese and goat cheese now. I seem to crave anything full fat.

Nowadays - a cheat day is an apple or some Stilton and red wine and I would probably have sushi if I was out with a friend.
That certailey is light years away from where I used to be.....so grateful....and I love it as well - so satisfying.

# Thread: Mayonnaise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### endless — 2013-03-20T22:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81217>

Anyone have a good mayo recipe? I can't find anything without canola or soybean oil in the stores. I was toying with the idea of using MCT oil....anyone tried that?

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T22:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81218>

[http://nomnompaleo.com/post/3440774534/paleo-mayonnaise](http://nomnompaleo.com/post/3440774534/paleo-mayonnaise)

### diane — 2013-03-20T22:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81220>

This one's really good. With olive oil.

[http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/08/19/mayo-how-to-video/](http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/08/19/mayo-how-to-video/)

### Priscilla — 2013-03-21T00:39:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81233>

I make mine similar to the nomnompaleo link (above). I use avocado oil, and cider vinegar (with the mother) instead of white vinegar. I also do it with an immersion blender. Put all the ingredients except the oil in the container, then add oil on top, then place the immersion blender on the bottom. The oil incorporates quickly. slowly raise the blender to finish incorporating it all.

This is so simple. But.. when you are done, _yank the cord from the outlet first_! Not doing this brought me an emergency room visit and 10 stitches on the tip of my finger!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-21T01:06:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81239>

Priscilla, I'm going to try your method. I used to make mayo a lot but got lazy and stopped several years ago. Tried to make it last year and twice it flopped due to me not dropping the oil in slowly enough. I love the immersion blender idea! Thanks!

### endless — 2013-03-21T18:53:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81332>

Avocado oil! Duh.....why didn't I think of that, it's perfect! Thanks everyone!

### CarolMorris — 2013-03-21T18:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81333>

With the immersion blender it's like...magic

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-21T19:38:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81340>

### Rachelle — 2013-03-21T21:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81349>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=81332)

> Avocado oil! Duh.....why didn't I think of that, it's perfect! Thanks everyone!

### Rachelle — 2013-03-21T21:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81350>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=81340)
>
> I can't the mayo to set for the life of me... ends up very runny - even when I follow directions to a T as far as measurements... doesn't matter if I use an emersion blender, my vitamix, kitchen aid hand mixer, or whipping by hand... total failure...what am I doing wrong????

Same here at Shijin, until I used my food processor. It made the difference for me. Just put in egg yoks, vinegar and salt and then start the food processor to make yolks to incorporate with vinegar and salt for a few seconds. Then slowly pour in oil while keeping the food processor running. Works every time.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-30T04:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82243>

MCT would probably work fine. You could add some olive, avocado, or macadamia oil in there to vary the flavor some. MCT is a very "runny" oil. I plan to make some homemade mayo soon to use with a salmon, chopped clam, onion, and cucumber salad similar to a tuna salad. Maybe I will add some chopped boiled egg. It probably won't be pretty to look at.

Start with slow bursts of the stick blender at the bottom of the jar. Don't raise the stick blender higher until obvious mayo is beginning to form at the bottom. Egg yolk has the lecithin that emulsifies the fat so use enough egg yolk for the job.

### Glamazon — 2013-03-30T07:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82253>

I have used half olive and half MCT oil. I think with the new batch I am going all MCT oil. I use a little cider vinegar. You can also making it with bacon grease. Haven't done that yet. You can also use lemon juice instead of cider vinegar. Gives it a very bright, light taste. I have also added some chopped garlic and chives.

### diane — 2013-03-30T15:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82274>

Yes, I use lemon juice. I've tried with fresh lemon juice, but read that you get better results with bottled. Which I've found helps.

[https://www.vitacost.com/chosen-foods-classic-mayo-100-avocado-oil-based?CSRC=BPA-815074020096-bing\_pla\_gen\_pro\_food+&+beverages-&network=s&keywordname=&device=c&adid=92700074207528873&matchtype=e&gclick=&ds\_agid=58700008164553767&targetid=&utm\_source=bing&utm\_medium=cpc&utm\_campaign=bing\_pla\_gen\_pro\_food & beverages&utm\_term=4580496736182091&utm\_content=bing\_gen\_pro\_food & beverages\_condiments&gclsrc=ds&gclsrc=ds](https://www.vitacost.com/chosen-foods-classic-mayo-100-avocado-oil-based?CSRC=BPA-815074020096-bing_pla_gen_pro_food+%26+beverages-&network=s&keywordname=&device=c&adid=92700074207528873&matchtype=e&gclick=&ds_agid=58700008164553767&targetid=&utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=bing_pla_gen_pro_food%20%26%20beverages&utm_term=4580496736182091&utm_content=bing_gen_pro_food%20%26%20beverages_condiments&gclsrc=ds&gclsrc=ds)

### Marko Pollo — 2023-03-19T18:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-320079>

After roasting and eating a duck or chicken, simmer till broth is how you like and let it cool off. Skim, strain and separate the fat. It will be soft at room temp. Make mayo with this. Doesn't last long but is real food, not some virtue signaling processed garbage claiming the health benefits of processed oils. This will also make amazing blue cheese dressing if you like that. We have store bought mayo in the fridge, but it is not in the same league in terms of flavor or nutritional density. If the consistency doesn't perfectly mimic the commercial stuff, remember the food chemists can and do use any substance to make the product appear more appetizing and shelf stable.

### Annemarie — 2023-05-05T19:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-320912>

You can make mayonaise with all kinds of fat, with butter, or baconaise with bacon fat. Or if it has to be very neutral I guess you could use the flavourless coconut oill as well.

### ab70bandit — 2023-05-31T22:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-321933>

The problem with alot of homemade butter mayo recipes is that after it's been in the fridge the mayo is not that spreadable. Chris cooking Nashville solve that issue with this recipe....
**Spreadable** butter mayo recipe: 1 large egg (separated) 1-2 tsp apple cider vinegar (optional) 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional) 1 pinch salt 1 stick butter (melted; 140-145F) Place egg yolk, vinegar, mustard, and pinch of salt in narrow container. Use stick blender on medium speed to combine well (10-15 seconds). Add melted butter 1-2 Tbsp at a time, blending well 10-15 seconds each time with stick blender in between each addition. This will create a thick mayonnaise. Add additional salt to taste if desired. Add egg white on top of thick mayo. Blend 5-10 seconds to combine. Blend an additional 10 seconds on high speed. Mayo will be a little frothy and somewhat runny. Refrigerate and use after it sets up. Mayo should be soft and spreadable straight out of the fridge.

# Thread: Seaweeds have arrived/Don't know how much of what to take
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweeds-have-arrived-dont-know-how-much-of-what-to-take.6414/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### Claudia — 2013-03-20T22:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweeds-have-arrived-dont-know-how-much-of-what-to-take.6414/post-81221>

I purchased some seaweeds and hoping to eventually get off t3 medication. I've purchased Kelp and Bladderwrack capsules from Mountain Herbs , Each capsule of Organic Kelp contains 595mg of kelp and the bladderwrack has 575 mg of organic bladderwrack along with this message: This product contains chemicals known to the state of California to cause cancer or birth defects or other reproductive harm.

How much do I take? Just 1 per day.

I also have wakame and irish moss but they are loose and not capsules. I was going to sprinkle some in water and just drink. Again, I have no idea how much to take?

Should I not take all of these at once? I wish I had some guidelines.

### Hope — 2013-03-21T02:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweeds-have-arrived-dont-know-how-much-of-what-to-take.6414/post-81254>

I think the kelp is good one a day, to start? And the other too? You could empty the caps and pour it over food? I would titrate up slowly, start with a small amount of whatever you do.....so your body has a chance to adjust to all that great nutrition you're giving it. I remember Dr K mentioning in a webinar that as you use sea veggies, the diet etc, you might get some heart racing/faster beats, etc, which he said might tell you you're optimizing T3 levels and showing you you can back off. THat's what happened to me (including a 10 lb weight loss!), the only thing is...it also made my TSH go really low and I had to back off and I still haven't found the right dose. BUT that's my N=1, hopefully it will be much easier/smoothly for you!

Good luck.....

### cloak — 2013-03-21T01:38:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81244>

I've been reading around here and finally decided to join. I also know MamaGrok and have been encouraged by the progress she has made using the info from this site. I'm a mid 30's female with hashimotos/urticaria and I need to figure out how to get my hives under control. Was diagnosed Hashimoto's when pregnant with kid #2 but it wasn't until I developed chronic hives after kid #3 that I saw a naturopath and learned about being gluten free amongst a lot of other things. I was doing well for awhile and then my hives came back full force. Happy to say that all my dietary changes have gotten my autoantibodies well in the normal range but I still have these awful hives! Looking forward to learning what I can from the wise folks on this forum.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T02:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81246>

I cloak, glad to have you here. Hives are tough to live with. I have had them as an allergic reaction.

### diane — 2013-03-21T02:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81251>

Welcome cloak! Great to hear about your progress, and I hope you can find some help for your hives. That can't be pleasant to live with.

### cloak — 2013-03-21T05:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81268>

Thanks, everyone.

Nonchalant, I'm just about everything-free right now it seems. The only grain I eat is rice (the one non-paleo food I'm holding out on) and I'm free of all the AI foods except nightshades and I do eat eggs here and there. I'm off of a whole list of other stuff from my IgG testing. I also can't eat tomatoes, cinnamon, and parsnips because they flare up my psoriasis. Weeding flares it up to and I'm not sure what that is all about. Spring is just bad in general because I'm highly allergic to tree pollens so everything goes out of whack when those are out.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-21T14:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81288>

hi cloak....I've been dealing with histamine and hives off and on throughout my life....I feel your itch as I write this!! I had loads of triggers that all make sense to me now. The last bout I had was my reaction to the antidepressant Elavil a few years ago. It was the clearest way my body could give me the message to get the hell off of these drugs. I had full blown full body hives for six weeks....horrific.

Anyway....If you haven't read the histamine thread, you might find some helpful stuff there and I would encourage you to simplify your diet even more. I've done really well on the seafoods I can tolerate (basically it has to alive (oysters) or eaten right away after cooking (clams, mussels lobster etc). I can't eat canned foods at all or fish that is over a day old.... I eat lots of organ meats, fats, muscle meat and almost no veggies. This has served me very well for the last year. I will be adding some more veggies this summer...but I'm planning on sticking with this for a long time. I need to repair a lot of bone and electron losses.

Have you tried taking lots of quercetin? That's been useful for me too. I believe sleeping grounded has made a huge difference in my allergies...I'm am no longer sneezing every single morning of every day. Feels great to just get up and breath normally.

Soo...lots to process, but I would gently suggest that you might benefit from just giving your body a real rest and lose the rice, the nightshades, eggs and eat really simply and drink amazing amounts of good water, stay away from EMFs and try to ground as much as possible.

Another avenue to read up on is how histamine fits into the methylation cycle...a couple of good threads on that too. Sorry if this is overload...I'm glad you're here and hope you find what you need. All the best, SeaHorse

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T14:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.6416/post-81290>

Cloak, you need some energy.
Sounds like going out into the sun would just aggravate your allergies at this point, though. Yeah, tree pollens are the worst for me, even worse than grass & weed pollens.
I think you should seriously consider grounding. It got rid of the rash I had on the back of my hand. I had it for a year. I think it was psoriasis or eczema. When it was at its worst, it would be swollen, red, and get a silvery dandruff-like covering. I learned to control it by dunking the hand in water with epsom salts, sea salt, and iodine, and then after air drying, putting olive oil on it.

But what finally got rid of the rash was when I started serious grounding at night. Took about a month.

So I have had two MD visits in the last two weeks and at both, it appears my blood pressure was all of 85 over 55. This is the lowest it's ever been for me....usually from the nervousness of being at the MD office, it's around 120/80, and when I'm with an MD I like, and at home, it's usually been 100 over 70.

I am healing too from AF and leaky gut...making some progress (waiting for DHEA results, my Vit D (89) and HDL (90) has been OK this month) though my thyroid is acting up when I go off of T3 support.

Anyone else ever seen this? Any idea what might cause it? More CT? More DHA and iodine in my diet?

Not sure I should be concerned but the one MD kept asking me if I was dizzy, which I'm not...but I am tired....a lot but no different than before.

### jamsrich — 2013-04-12T13:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-dropping.6417/post-83875>

Do exercise daily and plan our daily diet to requirement of your disease..

### MeghanK — 2013-04-13T17:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-dropping.6417/post-83998>

I have BP of 80/50 ish never seen it higher than 94/55 or something like that..... I also have a super low resting HR 40.... but the MDs say it's because I'm an endurance athlete... wonder what Barry's BP is?...... BUT I also know that anorexia causes low BP and resting HR too.....

### paleo-bunny — 2013-04-13T19:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-dropping.6417/post-84000>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=82669)
>
> Can you explain this? Wax lyrical? I'm thankful that I am not dizzy. Weak/tired I feel anyway...no matter what the BP, lower or normal

Low BP is trivialised big time.

I've normalised my blood pressure by:

\- getting my mercury amalgam fillings replaced
\- eating an anti-inflammatory, low-histamine diet

My blood pressure went up and normalised as I lost weight.

I still experience the odd bout of low blood pressure. Quercetin, salt and caffeine help.
